Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/flockdesk/core/services/profile_service.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nFlockDesk \u2013 Social Workspace Orchestrator\ncore.services.profile_service\n\nThis module provides the high-level \u201cProfileService\u201d that manages the user\u2019s\nroaming profile, including local persistence, remote cloud sync, and in-process\npublish / subscribe to profile-related events.\n\nThe service fulfils several responsibilities:\n\n1.  Persist a *single-source-of-truth* profile document to disk so the desktop\n    boots instantly \u2013 even without network connectivity.\n2.  Keep the local copy in lock-step with the cloud copy via an incremental,\n    fault-tolerant background synchronisation loop.\n3.  Broadcast profile changes on the application-wide event-bus so that\n    micro-front-ends (chat, whiteboard, etc.) can react to preference changes\n    in real-time without tight coupling.\n4.  Offer a simple observer interface (`register_listener`) for plug-ins that\n    prefer a direct callback instead of routing through the event bus.\n\nThe implementation is deliberately *thread-safe* and *resilient* \u2013 any\nunexpected exception is caught and reported without bringing down the hosting\nprocess.\n\nTypical usage\n-------------\n```python\nfrom flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus\nfrom flockdesk.core.services.profile_service import ProfileService\n\nevent_bus = EventBus()\nprofile_service = ProfileService(event_bus, user_id=\"42\")\nprofile_service.start()          # kick-off background sync\n\n# Retrieve the currently cached profile\nprofile = profile_service.profile  \n\n# Update the profile\nprofile.preferences[\"theme\"] = \"dark\"\nprofile_service.save(profile)    # persist+sync change\n```\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport pathlib\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, MutableMapping, Optional\n\nimport requests\n\n# External dependencies \u2013 these modules are expected to live inside the\n# FlockDesk code base.  We use `type: ignore` to keep linters quiet when this\n# file is opened standalone.\nfrom flockdesk.core.event_bus import Event, EventBus  # type: ignore\n\nLOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)\nDEFAULT_CLOUD_ENDPOINT = \"https://api.flockdesk.cloud/v1/profile\"\n\n_JSON_INDENT = 2  # pretty-print profile on disk for human readability\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass UserProfile:\n    \"\"\"Serializable user profile document.\n\n    Additional keys are supported via `extra` so plug-ins can store arbitrary\n    information without waiting for the core service to evolve.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    username: str\n    preferences: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    layout: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    last_updated_ts: float = field(default_factory=lambda: time.time())\n    extra: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_json(cls, data: MutableMapping[str, Any]) -> \"UserProfile\":\n        \"\"\"Create a `UserProfile` from a JSON-decoded dict.\"\"\"\n        return cls(\n            user_id=data[\"user_id\"],\n            username=data.get(\"username\", \"\"),\n            preferences=data.get(\"preferences\", {}),\n            layout=data.get(\"layout\", {}),\n            last_updated_ts=data.get(\"last_updated_ts\", time.time()),\n            extra=data.get(\"extra\", {}),\n        )\n\n    def to_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Return a JSON-serialisable mapping.\"\"\"\n        return asdict(self)\n\n\nclass StorageProvider:\n    \"\"\"Simple JSON-file storage for the user profile.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, base_path: Optional[pathlib.Path] = None) -> None:\n        self._base_path = (\n            base_path if base_path is not None else pathlib.Path.home() / \".config\" / \"FlockDesk\"\n        )\n        self._base_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # public\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def load(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[UserProfile]:\n        path = self._file_path(user_id)\n        if not path.exists():\n            return None\n        try:\n            data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n            return UserProfile.from_json(data)\n        except Exception:\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to load profile from disk: %s\", path)\n            return None\n\n    def save(self, profile: UserProfile) -> None:\n        path = self._file_path(profile.user_id)\n        try:\n            path.write_text(\n                json.dumps(profile.to_json(), indent=_JSON_INDENT),\n                encoding=\"utf-8\",\n            )\n        except Exception:\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to save profile to disk: %s\", path)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _file_path(self, user_id: str) -> pathlib.Path:\n        return self._base_path / f\"profile_{user_id}.json\"\n\n\nclass ProfileService:\n    \"\"\"High-level facade around local/remote profile management.\"\"\"\n\n    # pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        event_bus: EventBus,\n        user_id: str,\n        *,\n        storage: Optional[StorageProvider] = None,\n        remote_endpoint: str = DEFAULT_CLOUD_ENDPOINT,\n        sync_interval_s: int = 120,\n        max_backoff_s: int = 10 * 60,\n        session: Optional[requests.Session] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._bus = event_bus\n        self._user_id = user_id\n        self._storage = storage or StorageProvider()\n        self._remote_endpoint = remote_endpoint.rstrip(\"/\")\n        self._sync_interval = max(15, sync_interval_s)  # sane lower bound\n        self._max_backoff = max_backoff_s\n        self._session = session or requests.Session()\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n\n        self._listeners: List[Callable[[UserProfile], None]] = []\n        self._profile: UserProfile = (\n            self._storage.load(user_id) or self._initialise_blank_profile()\n        )\n\n        self._sync_thread = threading.Thread(\n            target=self._sync_loop,\n            name=\"FlockDeskProfileSync\",\n            daemon=True,\n        )\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n\n        # subscribe to in-process events\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"profile/refresh\", self._on_external_refresh)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # lifecycle\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the background sync loop.\"\"\"\n        if not self._sync_thread.is_alive():\n            LOG.debug(\"Starting ProfileService sync thread for user %s\", self._user_id)\n            self._sync_thread.start()\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Signal the background thread to terminate and wait for completion.\"\"\"\n        LOG.debug(\"Stopping ProfileService sync thread for user %s\", self._user_id)\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        self._sync_thread.join(timeout=5)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # public \u2013 profile CRUD\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    @property\n    def profile(self) -> UserProfile:\n        with self._lock:\n            return self._profile\n\n    def save(self, profile: UserProfile, *, broadcast: bool = True) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist and broadcast a profile that has been modified in-memory.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            profile.last_updated_ts = time.time()\n            self._profile = profile\n            self._storage.save(profile)\n\n        if broadcast:\n            self._bus.publish(Event(\"profile/updated\", payload=profile.to_json()))\n            self._notify_listeners(profile)\n\n    def update_preferences(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Convenience helper to modify `preferences`.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            self._profile.preferences.update(kwargs)\n            self._profile.last_updated_ts = time.time()\n            self._storage.save(self._profile)\n\n        self._bus.publish(Event(\"profile/updated\", payload=self._profile.to_json()))\n        self._notify_listeners(self._profile)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # observer API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def register_listener(self, fn: Callable[[UserProfile], None]) -> Callable[[], None]:\n        \"\"\"Register a callback that fires when the profile changes.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        Callable[[], None]\n            Unregister function.\n        \"\"\"\n        if fn not in self._listeners:\n            self._listeners.append(fn)\n\n        def _unregister() -> None:\n            try:\n                self._listeners.remove(fn)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n        return _unregister\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # private \u2013 event bus hooks\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _on_external_refresh(self, event: Event) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Handle 'profile/refresh' emitted by external components.\"\"\"\n        LOG.debug(\"Received external profile refresh request event=%s\", event.meta)\n        # Force an immediate remote sync outside of the normal interval\n        threading.Thread(\n            target=self._pull_remote, name=\"FlockDeskProfileForcePull\", daemon=True\n        ).start()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # private \u2013 synchronisation loop\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _sync_loop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Background loop \u2013 push/pull profile with exponential backoff.\"\"\"\n        backoff_s = self._sync_interval\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            start = time.time()\n            try:\n                self._push_local()    # push first so remote gets our changes\n                self._pull_remote()   # then pull remote updates\n                backoff_s = self._sync_interval  # reset backoff after success\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 \u2013 catch-all keeps the loop alive\n                LOG.exception(\"Profile sync failed \u2013 backing off\")\n                backoff_s = min(backoff_s * 2, self._max_backoff)\n            finally:\n                elapsed = time.time() - start\n                sleep_time = max(backoff_s - elapsed, 1)\n                self._stop_event.wait(timeout=sleep_time)\n\n    def _push_local(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Push local profile to the cloud if it has diverged.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            payload = self._profile.to_json()\n\n        url = f\"{self._remote_endpoint}/{self._user_id}\"\n        try:\n            res = self._session.put(url, json=payload, timeout=10)\n            res.raise_for_status()\n            LOG.debug(\"Pushed profile to remote (%s)\", res.status_code)\n        except requests.RequestException:\n            LOG.warning(\"Failed to push profile to cloud\", exc_info=True)\n            raise\n\n    def _pull_remote(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Pull profile from cloud and merge if newer than local.\"\"\"\n        url = f\"{self._remote_endpoint}/{self._user_id}\"\n        try:\n            res = self._session.get(url, timeout=10)\n            res.raise_for_status()\n            remote_doc = res.json()\n        except requests.RequestException:\n            LOG.info(\"Profile pull skipped \u2013 network unavailable\")\n            raise\n\n        remote_profile = UserProfile.from_json(remote_doc)\n        with self._lock:\n            if remote_profile.last_updated_ts > self._profile.last_updated_ts:\n                LOG.debug(\"Remote profile is newer \u2013 updating local copy\")\n                self._profile = remote_profile\n                self._storage.save(remote_profile)\n\n        # Publish regardless \u2013 the event bus de-duplicates on payload hash\n        self._bus.publish(Event(\"profile/updated\", payload=remote_profile.to_json()))\n        self._notify_listeners(remote_profile)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _notify_listeners(self, profile: UserProfile) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invoke registered callbacks (best-effort, non-blocking).\"\"\"\n\n        def _run(fn: Callable[[UserProfile], None]) -> None:\n            try:\n                fn(profile)\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                LOG.exception(\"Profile listener raised\")\n\n        for listener in list(self._listeners):  # shallow copy to avoid mutation\n            threading.Thread(target=_run, args=(listener,), daemon=True).start()\n\n    def _initialise_blank_profile(self) -> UserProfile:\n        \"\"\"Create a default profile document for first-time users.\"\"\"\n        LOG.info(\"Creating new blank profile for user %s\", self._user_id)\n        profile = UserProfile(\n            user_id=self._user_id,\n            username=f\"user-{self._user_id}\",\n            preferences={\n                \"theme\": \"light\",\n                \"language\": \"en\",\n            },\n            layout={},\n            extra={},\n        )\n        self._storage.save(profile)\n        return profile\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/core/services/profile_service.py",
    "numLines": 350,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 350
  },
  "type": "text"
}
12. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/service.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.service\n=================================\n\nPresenceService is responsible for tracking the local user\u2019s presence\nstatus, broadcasting updates to the internal event-bus, and consuming\npresence-events from other peers so that the rest of the desktop suite\n(i.e. chat panes, user lists, dashboards) can present up-to-date\navailability information.\n\nKey responsibilities\n--------------------\n1. Detect local activity / idleness and derive an appropriate\n   `PresenceState`.\n2. Expose an explicit `set_status` API so plugins (e.g. \u201cDo-Not-Disturb\n   scheduler\u201d) or GUI controls can override the automatic heuristic.\n3. Maintain an in-memory registry of *all* known users\u2019 presence data.\n4. Publish `presence.updated` events and subscribe to the same topic for\n   remote updates\u2014guaranteeing eventual consistency across processes.\n5. Provide a coroutine-driven lifecycle that can be started / stopped\n   from the application\u2019s service-loader infrastructure.\n\nThe implementation purposely avoids any hard dependency on a concrete\nEventBus or IPC layer.  Instead, it relies on a lightweight protocol\ninterface (`EventBusProtocol`) so that different deployments (embedded\nin-proc, ZeroMQ, or an HTTP bridge) can supply their own bus adapter.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport enum\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom types import TracebackType\nfrom typing import (\n    Any,\n    Awaitable,\n    Callable,\n    Dict,\n    MutableMapping,\n    Optional,\n    Protocol,\n    Type,\n)\n\nLOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Protocols & Data Models\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass EventBusProtocol(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    A minimal protocol any event-bus implementation has to satisfy so the\n    PresenceService can remain transport-agnostic.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self, topic: str, callback: Callable[[str, Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]\n    ) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n        ...\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        ...\n\n\nclass PresenceState(str, enum.Enum):\n    \"\"\"Enumeration of all presence states supported by FlockDesk.\"\"\"\n\n    ONLINE = \"online\"\n    AWAY = \"away\"\n    DND = \"dnd\"  # Do-Not-Disturb\n    OFFLINE = \"offline\"\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        return self.value\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass PresenceInfo:\n    \"\"\"\n    Canonical presence payload that gets serialized to the event-bus.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    state: PresenceState\n    updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    # Free-form metadata for plugins (e.g. custom emoji, busy_reason)\n    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            \"user_id\": self.user_id,\n            \"state\": self.state.value,\n            \"updated_at\": self.updated_at.isoformat(),\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_json(cls, raw: Dict[str, Any]) -> \"PresenceInfo\":\n        return cls(\n            user_id=raw[\"user_id\"],\n            state=PresenceState(raw[\"state\"]),\n            updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(raw[\"updated_at\"]),\n            metadata=raw.get(\"metadata\", {}),\n        )\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Helper Utilities\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ndef _system_idle_seconds() -> float:\n    \"\"\"\n    Cross-platform(ish) API for fetching idle time. We degrade gracefully to\n    returning `0` when platform integration is unavailable rather than\n    raising\u2014presence detection will still work albeit less smart.\n\n    Notes\n    -----\n    \u2022 Windows:   GetLastInputInfo via `ctypes`.\n    \u2022 macOS:     IOKit wrapper (\u274c not yet implemented).\n    \u2022 X11/Wayland:  XScreenSaver (\u274c not yet implemented).\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n            import ctypes  # pylint: disable=import-error\n\n            class LASTINPUTINFO(ctypes.Structure):  # type: ignore\n                _fields_ = [(\"cbSize\", ctypes.c_uint), (\"dwTime\", ctypes.c_uint)]\n\n            plii = LASTINPUTINFO()\n            plii.cbSize = ctypes.sizeof(plii)\n            if ctypes.windll.user32.GetLastInputInfo(ctypes.byref(plii)) == 0:  # type: ignore  # noqa\n                return 0.0\n            millis = ctypes.windll.kernel32.GetTickCount() - plii.dwTime  # type: ignore\n            return millis / 1000.0\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Idle time detection failed\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Unsupported OS or error\u2014fallback.\n    return 0.0\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Presence Service\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass PresenceService:\n    \"\"\"\n    High-level fa\u00e7ade exposed to the rest of the desktop suite.\n\n    Lifecycle (`async with PresenceService(...):`) is preferred so that the\n    activity watchdog coroutine shuts down cleanly during application exit.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = timedelta(seconds=30)\n    IDLE_THRESHOLD = timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        user_id: str,\n        bus: EventBusProtocol,\n        idle_provider: Callable[[], float] | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._user_id: str = user_id\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._idle_provider = idle_provider or _system_idle_seconds\n\n        self._registry: MutableMapping[str, PresenceInfo] = {}\n        self._local_state: PresenceState = PresenceState.ONLINE\n        self._override_state: PresenceState | None = None\n\n        # Concurrency\n        self._loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n        self._tasks: set[asyncio.Task[Any]] = set()\n        self._stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n\n        # Register bus subscription early so we don't miss events during `await __aenter__`\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.updated\", self._on_presence_event)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Context Manager Helpers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> \"PresenceService\":\n        self.start()\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(\n        self,\n        exc_type: Type[BaseException] | None,\n        exc: BaseException | None,\n        tb: TracebackType | None,\n    ) -> Optional[bool]:\n        await self.stop()\n        # Don't suppress any exception\n        return None\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Spawn background coroutines (watchdog + heartbeat).\"\"\"\n        if self._tasks:\n            return  # Already started\n        LOGGER.debug(\"PresenceService starting\")\n        self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._activity_watchdog()))\n        self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._heartbeat_publisher()))\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Signal background tasks to stop and wait for graceful exit.\"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"PresenceService stopping\")\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        # Wait for tasks to finish with timeout\n        await asyncio.wait(self._tasks, timeout=2.0)\n        self._tasks.clear()\n\n    async def set_status(\n        self,\n        state: PresenceState,\n        *,\n        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n        override: bool = True,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Explicitly set the local user\u2019s status.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        state:\n            One of PresenceState values.\n        metadata:\n            Optional free-form metadata blob.\n        override:\n            If `True` automatic idle detection is disabled until\n            `clear_manual_override()` is called.  This prevents race\n            conditions when the user explicitly picks *DND* but is\n            technically \u201cactive\u201d according to the heuristic.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._override_state = state if override else None\n        await self._broadcast_local_state(state, metadata or {})\n\n    async def clear_manual_override(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Re-enable automatic presence detection that may have been\n        suspended via `set_status(..., override=True)`.\n        \"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence manual override cleared\")\n        self._override_state = None\n        # Force immediate reconsideration\n        await self._evaluate_local_state()\n\n    def get_presence(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[PresenceInfo]:\n        \"\"\"Return the latest presence data we have for `user_id`.\"\"\"\n        return self._registry.get(user_id)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Internal \u2013 Background Tasks\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def _activity_watchdog(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Periodically check system idle time and update `self._local_state`\n        accordingly.\n        \"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence watchdog started\")\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                await self._evaluate_local_state()\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                LOGGER.exception(\"Presence watchdog error\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(5)  # Poll frequency\n\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence watchdog terminated\")\n\n    async def _heartbeat_publisher(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Consolidated heartbeat ensuring that *some* presence update is sent\n        at least every `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL`, even if the state hasn\u2019t\n        changed.  This aids recovery in case any bus message was missed.\n        \"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence heartbeat started\")\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                await self._broadcast_local_state(self._local_state, {})\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                LOGGER.exception(\"Presence heartbeat error\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(self.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL.total_seconds())\n\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence heartbeat terminated\")\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Internal \u2013 State Evaluation & Publishing\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def _evaluate_local_state(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Decide which `PresenceState` the local user should be in based on\n        idle time and manual overrides.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Manual override takes precedence\n        if self._override_state:\n            state = self._override_state\n        else:\n            idle_seconds = self._idle_provider()\n            state = (\n                PresenceState.AWAY\n                if idle_seconds >= self.IDLE_THRESHOLD.total_seconds()\n                else PresenceState.ONLINE\n            )\n\n        if state != self._local_state:\n            LOGGER.debug(\n                \"Presence state transition: %s \u2192 %s (override=%s)\",\n                self._local_state,\n                state,\n                bool(self._override_state),\n            )\n            await self._broadcast_local_state(state, {})\n\n    async def _broadcast_local_state(\n        self, state: PresenceState, metadata: Dict[str, Any]\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update internal registry and publish the change.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._local_state = state\n        payload = PresenceInfo(\n            user_id=self._user_id,\n            state=state,\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n        # Update local registry synchronously\n        self._registry[self._user_id] = payload\n        await self._bus.publish(\"presence.updated\", payload.to_json())\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Event-Bus Handlers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def _on_presence_event(self, topic: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Called for *all* presence.updated events\u2014including the one we just\n        broadcast ourselves.  We update our registry and forward the\n        information to any interested observers (plugins, UI model, etc.).\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            info = PresenceInfo.from_json(data)\n        except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:\n            LOGGER.warning(\"Malformed presence payload: %s (err=%s)\", data, exc)\n            return\n\n        # Ignore outdated events \u2013 ensure monotonic update per user\n        current = self._registry.get(info.user_id)\n        if current and info.updated_at <= current.updated_at:\n            return\n\n        self._registry[info.user_id] = info\n        LOGGER.debug(\n            \"Presence updated: %s state=%s meta=%s\",\n            info.user_id,\n            info.state,\n            json.dumps(info.metadata, ensure_ascii=False),\n        )\n\n        # Fan-out to local observers by publishing an *internal* asyncio.Event.\n        # A more sophisticated implementation might rely on RxPy, signal\n        # dispatchers, or other observer patterns.  For now we stick with the\n        # event-bus only.\n        # NOTE: Nothing to do here; UI layers will subscribe to the same bus\n        # topic, or they can query `get_presence()` directly.\n\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/service.py",
    "numLines": 374,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 374
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/flockdesk/shared/schemas/user_profile.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.shared.schemas.user_profile\n====================================\n\nStrongly-typed, versioned schema for persisting a user\u2019s profile in FlockDesk.\nThe schema is expressed as nested Pydantic models to guarantee integrity,\nprovide automatic (de)serialization, and enable painless migrations when the\nstructure evolves.\n\nThe profile encapsulates:\n    \u2022 Identity   \u2013 id, e-mail address, display name, avatar path\n    \u2022 Preferences \u2013 theme, shortcuts, window layout, enabled plug-ins\n    \u2022 Timestamps  \u2013 creation, last update, schema revision\n\nThe helper methods `save()` and `load()` make it trivial to persist and restore\nprofiles as UTF-8 JSON files.  When older documents are encountered, an\ninternal migration pipeline `_migrate()` upgrades them on-the-fly so the rest\nof the application can rely on the latest shape.\n\nThis module has *no* external runtime dependencies outside of `pydantic`,\n`uuid`, and the Python standard library.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\nfrom uuid import UUID, uuid4\n\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator, validator\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"ThemeSettings\",\n    \"ShortcutBinding\",\n    \"KeyboardPreferences\",\n    \"WindowGeometry\",\n    \"WindowLayout\",\n    \"PluginConfig\",\n    \"UserPreferences\",\n    \"UserProfile\",\n]\n\n###############################################################################\n# Low-level building blocks\n###############################################################################\n\n\nclass ThemeSettings(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Visual styling preferences.\n\n    Attributes\n    ----------\n    name\n        Human-readable name of the theme.\n    accent_color\n        Hex triplet to use for accent highlights (validated).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    name: str = Field(\"FlockDark\", description=\"Name of the currently used theme.\")\n    accent_color: str = Field(\n        \"#1abc9c\",\n        regex=r\"^#(?:[0-9a-fA-F]{3}){1,2}$\",\n        description=\"HEX RGB color (e.g. #RRGGBB) used for accent highlights.\",\n    )\n\n\nclass ShortcutBinding(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Represents one keyboard shortcut mapping.\n\n    The `keys` field accepts multi-stroke definitions, e.g. `['Ctrl+Shift+P']`.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    action: str = Field(..., description=\"Canonical action identifier.\")\n    keys: List[str] = Field(\n        ...,\n        min_items=1,\n        description=\"Key sequence(s) that trigger the action.\",\n    )\n\n\nclass KeyboardPreferences(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"Collection of user-defined keyboard shortcuts.\"\"\"\n\n    bindings: List[ShortcutBinding] = Field(\n        default_factory=list, description=\"All custom keyboard bindings.\"\n    )\n\n    def get_binding_for_action(self, action: str) -> Optional[ShortcutBinding]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Return the shortcut binding for a given action, if present.\n        \"\"\"\n        return next((b for b in self.bindings if b.action == action), None)\n\n\nclass WindowGeometry(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Geometry description for a single window or dock widget.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    x: int\n    y: int\n    width: int\n    height: int\n    is_maximized: bool = Field(\n        False, description=\"Whether the window was maximised when saved.\"\n    )\n\n    @validator(\"*\", pre=True)\n    def _ensure_int(cls, v: Any) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Pydantic sometimes passes `None` for missing fields, we make sure the\n        geometry is always an integer \u2265 0 to avoid Qt warnings later.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not isinstance(v, int) or v < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"Window geometry values must be non-negative integers.\")\n        return v\n\n\nclass WindowLayout(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Persisted layout for main and dock windows.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    main_window: WindowGeometry\n    dock_widgets: Dict[str, WindowGeometry] = Field(\n        default_factory=dict,\n        description=\"Mapping dock-widget-id -> geometry.\",\n    )\n\n\nclass PluginConfig(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Per-plug-in runtime state (enabled, custom settings, \u2026).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    id: str = Field(..., description=\"Fully-qualified plug-in identifier.\")\n    enabled: bool = Field(True, description=\"Is the plug-in enabled?\")\n    state: Dict[str, Any] = Field(\n        default_factory=dict,\n        description=\"Opaque blob saved/restored by the plug-in itself.\",\n    )\n\n\n###############################################################################\n# High-level aggregated preferences\n###############################################################################\n\n\nclass UserPreferences(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Root container for everything customisable by the user.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    theme: ThemeSettings = Field(default_factory=ThemeSettings)\n    keyboard: KeyboardPreferences = Field(default_factory=KeyboardPreferences)\n    layout: WindowLayout\n    plugins: List[PluginConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)\n    last_updated: datetime = Field(\n        default_factory=datetime.utcnow,\n        description=\"UTC timestamp when preferences changed last.\",\n    )\n\n    # Convenience helpers -----------------------------------------------------\n\n    def plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> Optional[PluginConfig]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch a plug-in configuration by id.\n        \"\"\"\n        return next((p for p in self.plugins if p.id == plugin_id), None)\n\n    def enable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Enable (or add) a plug-in.\n        \"\"\"\n        cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)\n        if cfg:\n            cfg.enabled = True\n        else:\n            self.plugins.append(PluginConfig(id=plugin_id, enabled=True))\n\n    def disable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Disable a plug-in if it exists.\n        \"\"\"\n        cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)\n        if cfg:\n            cfg.enabled = False\n\n\n###############################################################################\n# Top-level profile\n###############################################################################\n\n\nclass UserProfile(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Complete persisted user profile.\n\n    The model is intentionally *not* immutable \u2013 live components mutate it, and\n    we rely on Pydantic\u2019s `validate_assignment` to keep things consistent.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- Meta\n    schema_version: int = Field(\n        1,\n        const=True,\n        description=\"Monotonically increasing schema revision.\",\n    )\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Identity\n    user_id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)\n    email: str\n    display_name: str\n    avatar_path: Optional[str] = Field(\n        None,\n        description=\"File path or HTTPS URL to the avatar image.\",\n    )\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------- Preferences\n    preferences: UserPreferences = Field(\n        default_factory=lambda: UserPreferences(\n            layout=WindowLayout(\n                main_window=WindowGeometry(\n                    x=50,\n                    y=50,\n                    width=1280,\n                    height=800,\n                    is_maximized=False,\n                )\n            )\n        )\n    )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------- Audit\n    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Config\n    class Config:\n        validate_assignment = True\n        json_encoders = {datetime: lambda dt: dt.replace(tzinfo=None).isoformat()}\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------- Hooks\n    @validator(\"email\")\n    def _email_must_be_valid(cls, v: str) -> str:\n        if \"@\" not in v:\n            raise ValueError(\"Invalid e-mail address.\")\n        return v.strip().lower()\n\n    @root_validator\n    def _touch_updated_at(cls, values: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Whenever the model is (re-)validated we update the timestamp so callers\n        don\u2019t have to remember doing it themselves.\n        \"\"\"\n        values[\"updated_at\"] = datetime.utcnow()\n        return values\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Public\n    # Persistence helpers .....................................................\n\n    def save(self, path: str | Path) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Flush the profile to *path* as pretty-printed UTF-8 JSON.\n        \"\"\"\n        path = Path(path)\n        try:\n            path.write_text(self.json(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover\n            raise IOError(f\"Unable to write user profile to {path}: {exc}\") from exc\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: str | Path) -> \"UserProfile\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Load a profile from *path*, migrating older versions automatically.\n        \"\"\"\n        path = Path(path)\n        if not path.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"User profile not found: {path}\")\n\n        raw_json = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        data = json.loads(raw_json)\n\n        current_version = cls.__fields__[\"schema_version\"].default\n        stored_version = data.get(\"schema_version\", 0)\n        if stored_version != current_version:\n            data = cls._migrate(data, from_version=stored_version)\n\n        try:\n            return cls.parse_obj(data)\n        except ValidationError as exc:  # pragma: no cover\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Corrupted profile '{path}':\\n{exc}\"\n            ) from exc\n\n    # Migration pipeline ......................................................\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _migrate(payload: Dict[str, Any], *, from_version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Upgrade *payload* to the latest schema version.\n\n        Every `if` block is responsible for migrating *from* a specific older\n        revision *to* the immediate next one.\n        \"\"\"\n        migrated = dict(payload)  # shallow copy\n\n        if from_version < 1:\n            # \u2013\u2013\u2013 v0 \u279c v1 \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\n            #  \u2022 Ensure `display_name` exists (fallback to local-part of e-mail)\n            #\n            migrated.setdefault(\n                \"display_name\",\n                migrated.get(\"email\", \"User\").split(\"@\")[0].title(),\n            )\n            migrated[\"schema_version\"] = 1\n\n        return migrated\n\n    # Convenience API .........................................................\n\n    def update_preferences(self, **changes: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Patch top-level keys of :class:`UserPreferences`.\n\n        Examples\n        --------\n        >>> user.update_preferences(theme=ThemeSettings(name=\"Solarized\"))\n        \"\"\"\n        for key, value in changes.items():\n            if not hasattr(self.preferences, key):\n                raise AttributeError(f\"Unknown preference section: {key!r}\")\n            setattr(self.preferences, key, value)\n\n        # Re-validate to bump `updated_at`\n        self.__dict__[\"updated_at\"] = datetime.utcnow()\n        self.preferences.last_updated = self.updated_at\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/shared/schemas/user_profile.py",
    "numLines": 343,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 343
  },
  "type": "text"
}
14. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/model/user_status.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.model.user_status\n===========================================\n\nModel abstractions that represent a user\u2019s *presence* state inside FlockDesk.\nThe model is intentionally agnostic of the underlying transport/event-bus\nimplementation so it can be reused on the server as well as within micro-UIs.\n\nThe responsibilities of this module are:\n\n    \u2022 Offer a strict, exhaustive enumeration of valid presence states\n    \u2022 Provide a value-object (`UserStatus`) that encapsulates all status-related\n      metadata (state, message, timestamps, \u2026)\n    \u2022 Enforce *legal* state-transitions via a tiny state-machine\n    \u2022 Offer (de)serialization helpers for round-tripping over the event-bus\n    \u2022 Provide an Observer API so interested parties can subscribe to status\n      changes in a decoupled fashion\n\nThe code purposefully stays dependency-light (only stdlib) to keep the model\nportable across the frontend and backend code-bases.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport threading\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Final, Iterable, List, Mapping, MutableSet\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"PresenceState\",\n    \"IllegalStatusTransition\",\n    \"UserStatus\",\n]\n\n_LOGGER: Final = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass PresenceState(enum.Enum):\n    \"\"\"\n    Exhaustive list of presence states a user can be in.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    OFFLINE = \"offline\"          # The user is not connected\n    INVISIBLE = \"invisible\"      # Connected, but appears offline\n    ONLINE = \"online\"            # Active and available\n    AWAY = \"away\"                # Idle or temporarily away\n    DND = \"do_not_disturb\"       # Does not want to be interrupted\n    BUSY = \"busy\"                # Actively working, but may respond\n    PRESENTING = \"presenting\"    # Sharing screen / in focus-mode\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_str(cls, value: str) -> \"PresenceState\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Convert a string to a PresenceState. Case-insensitive.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return cls(value.lower())\n        except ValueError as exc:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Unknown presence state: {value!r}\") from exc\n\n\n# Legal state transitions ----------------------------------------------------\n\n\n_ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: Mapping[PresenceState, MutableSet[PresenceState]] = {\n    PresenceState.OFFLINE: {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE,\n        PresenceState.INVISIBLE,\n    },\n    PresenceState.INVISIBLE: {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE,\n        PresenceState.DND,\n        PresenceState.AWAY,\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE,\n    },\n    PresenceState.ONLINE: {\n        PresenceState.AWAY,\n        PresenceState.BUSY,\n        PresenceState.DND,\n        PresenceState.PRESENTING,\n        PresenceState.INVISIBLE,\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE,\n    },\n    PresenceState.AWAY: {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE,\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE,\n        PresenceState.INVISIBLE,\n    },\n    PresenceState.BUSY: {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE,\n        PresenceState.DND,\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE,\n    },\n    PresenceState.DND: {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE,\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE,\n    },\n    PresenceState.PRESENTING: {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE,\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE,\n    },\n}\n\n\nclass IllegalStatusTransition(RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when an attempt is made to switch to a state that is not legal.\n    \"\"\"\n\n\n# Observer pattern helpers ----------------------------------------------------\n\n\nObserver = Callable[[\"UserStatus\"], None]\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)\nclass _Metadata:\n    \"\"\"Container for fields we do *not* expose via asdict().\"\"\"\n\n    sequence: int = 0\n\n\n# Main model ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass UserStatus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Value-object carrying the status of a single user.\n\n    The object is *mutable* so that UI components can hold a shared reference\n    that reflects live changes. Thread-safety is guaranteed via an internal\n    re-entrant lock.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    state: PresenceState = PresenceState.OFFLINE\n    message: str = \"\"\n    last_changed: datetime = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc))\n\n    # Private fields ----------------------------------------------------------\n    _observers: List[Observer] = field(default_factory=list, init=False, repr=False)\n    _lock: threading.RLock = field(default_factory=threading.RLock, init=False, repr=False)\n    _meta: _Metadata = field(default_factory=_Metadata, init=False, repr=False)\n\n    # Public API --------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Status mutation                                                        #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def update(\n        self,\n        new_state: PresenceState,\n        /,\n        *,\n        message: str | None = None,\n        force: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Transition to *new_state*.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        new_state:\n            The desired presence state.\n        message:\n            Optional user-visible status-message (e.g. \u201cGrabbing lunch\u201d). ``None``\n            keeps the current message untouched, an *empty* string clears it.\n        force:\n            Skip the allowed-transition validation. Reserved for system-level\n            code (e.g. session-restore) that needs to restore an older snapshot\n            verbatim.\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            if not force and not self._is_transition_allowed(new_state):\n                raise IllegalStatusTransition(\n                    f\"{self.state.value} \u2794 {new_state.value} is not allowed.\"\n                )\n\n            self.state = new_state\n            if message is not None:\n                self.message = message\n\n            self.last_changed = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)\n            object.__setattr__(\n                self._meta,\n                \"sequence\",\n                self._meta.sequence + 1,\n            )\n\n        _LOGGER.debug(\n            \"User '%s' changed status to %s (seq=%d)\",\n            self.user_id,\n            new_state.value,\n            self._meta.sequence,\n        )\n        self._notify_observers()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Observer utilities                                                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def attach(self, cb: Observer, /) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe *cb* to future status changes. Duplicate registrations\n        are ignored.\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            if cb not in self._observers:\n                self._observers.append(cb)\n\n    def detach(self, cb: Observer, /) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Unsubscribe *cb*. Calls to unknown observers are ignored.\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            try:\n                self._observers.remove(cb)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Serialization                                                         #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Serialize the object into a JSON-compatible dict.\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            payload = asdict(self)\n            # Replace complex objects\n            payload[\"state\"] = self.state.value\n            payload[\"last_changed\"] = self.last_changed.isoformat()\n            # Strip private fields added by dataclasses.asdict()\n            payload.pop(\"_observers\", None)\n            payload.pop(\"_lock\", None)\n            payload.pop(\"_meta\", None)\n            payload[\"sequence\"] = self._meta.sequence\n            return payload\n\n    def to_json(self, *, pretty: bool = False) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Serialize the object to a JSON string.\n        \"\"\"\n        if pretty:\n            return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2, sort_keys=True)\n        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), separators=(\",\", \":\"))\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Static helpers                                                        #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_dict(cls, data: Mapping[str, Any], /) -> \"UserStatus\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Restore a *UserStatus* from the mapping created by :py:meth:`to_dict`.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            instance = cls(\n                user_id=str(data[\"user_id\"]),\n                state=PresenceState.from_str(data[\"state\"]),\n                message=data.get(\"message\", \"\"),\n                last_changed=datetime.fromisoformat(data[\"last_changed\"]),\n            )\n        except KeyError as exc:\n            raise ValueError(\"Malformed user-status payload (missing field)\") from exc\n\n        # Restore the monotonically increasing sequence number (private)\n        object.__setattr__(instance._meta, \"sequence\", int(data.get(\"sequence\", 0)))\n        return instance\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_json(cls, raw: str | bytes, /) -> \"UserStatus\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Restore a *UserStatus* from its JSON representation.\n        \"\"\"\n        return cls.from_dict(json.loads(raw))\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Misc. utilities                                                       #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def copy(self) -> \"UserStatus\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a deep copy that is detached from observers and locks.\n        \"\"\"\n        return UserStatus.from_dict(self.to_dict())\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Private helpers                                                       #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def _is_transition_allowed(self, new_state: PresenceState) -> bool:\n        return new_state in _ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS[self.state]\n\n    def _notify_observers(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Notify subscribers in a *thread-safe* manner \u2013 but call them *outside*\n        the lock to avoid re-entrancy deadlocks. A failing observer will be\n        logged and *detached* automatically so that one bad plugin does not\n        block further updates.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Snapshot list to keep iteration stable even when observers detach\n        with self._lock:\n            observers: Iterable[Observer] = tuple(self._observers)\n\n        for cb in observers:\n            try:\n                cb(self)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                _LOGGER.exception(\n                    \"Observer %r failed while processing status-change for '%s'. \"\n                    \"Observer has been removed.\",\n                    cb,\n                    self.user_id,\n                )\n                self.detach(cb)\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/model/user_status.py",
    "numLines": 326,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 326
  },
  "type": "text"
}
