{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.shared.widgets.avatar_widget\n--------------------------------------\n\nReusable widget that displays (and manages) a user\u2019s avatar picture with\nreal-time presence indication, theme awareness, drag-and-drop support and an\nextensible context-menu.\n\nThe widget is intended to be used throughout the FlockDesk desktop client:\nin chat lists, white-board collaborators, community dashboards, etc.\n\nTypical usage\n~~~~~~~~~~~~~\navatar = AvatarWidget(\n    user_id=\"42\",\n    event_bus=inject.instance(EventBus),          # optional\n    theme_manager=inject.instance(ThemeManager),  # optional\n)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport sys\nfrom collections import OrderedDict\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import (QEvent, QPoint, QRunnable, QSize, Qt, QThreadPool,\n                            QUrl, Signal, Slot)\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import (QAction, QContextMenuEvent, QDragEnterEvent,\n                           QDropEvent, QIcon, QImage, QMouseEvent, QPainter,\n                           QPixmap)\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (QFileDialog, QMenu, QMessageBox, QWidget)\n\n__all__ = [\"AvatarWidget\"]\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# (Optional) third-party FlockDesk services\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass _DummyBus:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for the real application-wide EventBus (if not imported).\"\"\"\n\n    def subscribe(self, *_a, **_kw):\n        pass\n\n    def publish(self, *_a, **_kw):\n        pass\n\n\nclass _DummyTheme:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for the real ThemeManager (hot-swappable themes).\"\"\"\n\n    def color(self, key: str, fallback: str = \"#CCCCCC\") -> str:\n        return fallback\n\n\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus\nexcept Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n    EventBus = _DummyBus  # type: ignore\n\n\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.theme import ThemeManager\nexcept Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n    ThemeManager = _DummyTheme  # type: ignore\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Pixmap caching helpers\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass _PixmapCache:\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small LRU cache that stores pixmaps keyed by absolute path.\n    Prevents re-decoding identical images when the avatar is shown in multiple\n    locations at once.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _MAX_ENTRIES = 32\n    _store: OrderedDict[str, QPixmap] = OrderedDict()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def get(cls, path: str) -> Optional[QPixmap]:\n        try:\n            pixmap = cls._store.pop(path)\n            cls._store[path] = pixmap  # move to end (most recently used)\n            return pixmap\n        except KeyError:\n            return None\n\n    @classmethod\n    def put(cls, path: str, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\n        cls._store[path] = pixmap\n        cls._store.move_to_end(path)\n        # evict least recently used\n        while len(cls._store) > cls._MAX_ENTRIES:\n            cls._store.popitem(last=False)\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Asynchronous loader used to decode / scale the avatar off the UI thread\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass _AvatarLoader(QRunnable):\n    \"\"\"\n    Decode and scale a QPixmap in a thread-pool to avoid UI jank when very\n    large images are supplied.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        path: str,\n        target_size: QSize,\n        on_loaded: \"Signal[QPixmap]\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._path = path\n        self._target_size = target_size\n        self._on_loaded = on_loaded\n\n    def run(self) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        try:\n            image = QImage(self._path)\n            if image.isNull():\n                raise ValueError(f\"Unable to load image: {self._path}\")\n\n            # Preserve aspect ratio, crop to square, then scale\n            side = min(image.width(), image.height())\n            cropped = image.copy(\n                (image.width() - side) // 2,\n                (image.height() - side) // 2,\n                side,\n                side,\n            )\n            scaled = cropped.scaled(\n                self._target_size,\n                Qt.KeepAspectRatio,\n                Qt.SmoothTransformation,\n            )\n            pixmap = QPixmap.fromImage(scaled)\n            _PixmapCache.put(self._path, pixmap)\n            self._on_loaded.emit(pixmap)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            log.exception(\"Failed to load avatar: %s\", self._path)\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Avatar widget\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass AvatarWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Displays a round avatar with an optional presence badge.\n\n    Signals\n    -------\n    clicked: Signal[None]\n        Emitted on left mouse click.\n    avatarChanged: Signal[str]\n        Emitted when the user changes his / her avatar locally.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    clicked = Signal()\n    avatarChanged = Signal(str)\n\n    _DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST = {\n        \"image/png\",\n        \"image/jpeg\",\n        \"image/webp\",\n        \"image/gif\",\n    }\n    _DEFAULT_SIZE = 64\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        user_id: str,\n        *,\n        size: int | None = None,\n        event_bus: Optional[EventBus] = None,\n        theme_manager: Optional[ThemeManager] = None,\n        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(parent)\n\n        self.setAcceptDrops(True)\n        self.setCursor(Qt.PointingHandCursor)\n        self.setFixedSize(QSize(size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE, size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE))\n\n        # dependencies\n        self._user_id = user_id\n        self._event_bus = event_bus or _DummyBus()\n        self._theme = theme_manager or _DummyTheme()\n\n        # runtime state\n        self._avatar_path: Optional[str] = None\n        self._pixmap: Optional[QPixmap] = None\n        self._presence: str = \"offline\"  # offline, online, busy, away \u2026\n        self._thread_pool = QThreadPool.globalInstance()\n\n        self._install_event_listeners()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public API\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def avatar(self) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return absolute path of current avatar (may be None).\"\"\"\n        return self._avatar_path\n\n    def presence(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the current presence key (online / busy / \u2026).\"\"\"\n        return self._presence\n\n    @Slot(str)\n    def set_avatar(self, path: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update the avatar from the given image path.\"\"\"\n        path = os.path.abspath(path)\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            log.warning(\"Avatar path does not exist: %s\", path)\n            return\n\n        self._avatar_path = path\n        cached = _PixmapCache.get(path)\n        if cached is not None:\n            self._pixmap = cached\n            self.update()\n        else:\n            loader = _AvatarLoader(\n                path=path,\n                target_size=self.size(),\n                on_loaded=self._on_async_loaded,\n            )\n            self._thread_pool.start(loader)\n\n    @Slot(str)\n    def set_presence(self, state: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update presence overlay, e.g. from the event-bus.\"\"\"\n        if state == self._presence:\n            return\n        self._presence = state\n        self.update()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Event handling\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def paintEvent(self, event):  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Paint circular avatar with presence badge.\"\"\"\n        painter = QPainter(self)\n        painter.setRenderHint(QPainter.Antialiasing)\n\n        if self._pixmap and not self._pixmap.isNull():\n            painter.setBrush(Qt.NoBrush)\n            path = self._rounded_rect_path()\n            painter.setClipPath(path)\n            painter.drawPixmap(self.rect(), self._pixmap)\n        else:\n            # fallback placeholder\n            painter.setBrush(\n                self._theme.color(\"avatar.background\", \"#8F8F8F\")\n            )\n            painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\n            painter.drawEllipse(self.rect())\n\n        self._draw_presence_indicator(painter)\n\n    def mousePressEvent(self, event: QMouseEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        if event.button() == Qt.LeftButton:\n            self.clicked.emit()\n        super().mousePressEvent(event)\n\n    def dragEnterEvent(self, event: QDragEnterEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        if any(fmt in event.mimeData().formats() for fmt in self._DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST):\n            event.acceptProposedAction()\n\n    def dropEvent(self, event: QDropEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        url: QUrl\n        for url in event.mimeData().urls():\n            if url.isLocalFile():\n                self._handle_new_avatar(Path(url.toLocalFile()))\n                event.acceptProposedAction()\n                return\n        QMessageBox.warning(self, \"Invalid file\", \"Please drop a local image file.\")\n\n    def contextMenuEvent(self, event: QContextMenuEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        menu = QMenu(self)\n\n        choose_action = QAction(\"Change avatar \u2026\", self)\n        choose_action.triggered.connect(self._choose_avatar)\n        menu.addAction(choose_action)\n\n        menu.addSeparator()\n\n        if self._avatar_path:\n            reveal = QAction(\"Reveal in Explorer\", self)\n            reveal.triggered.connect(self._reveal_in_file_manager)\n            menu.addAction(reveal)\n\n        menu.exec(event.globalPos())\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal helpers\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _install_event_listeners(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to application-wide events: theme change, presence updates\n        for our user, etc.\n        \"\"\"\n        # presence update\n        if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\n            self._event_bus.subscribe(\n                \"presence.changed\",\n                handler=lambda e: (\n                    self.set_presence(e.payload[\"state\"])\n                    if e.payload.get(\"user_id\") == self._user_id\n                    else None\n                ),\n            )\n\n        # theme hot reload\n        if isinstance(self._theme, ThemeManager):\n            self._theme.themeChanged.connect(lambda: self.update())  # type: ignore\n\n    @Slot(QPixmap)\n    def _on_async_loaded(self, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\n        self._pixmap = pixmap\n        self.update()\n\n    def _rounded_rect_path(self):\n        \"\"\"Returns a QPainterPath of a circle matching widget size.\"\"\"\n        from PySide6.QtGui import QPainterPath\n\n        path = QPainterPath()\n        path.addEllipse(self.rect())\n        return path\n\n    def _draw_presence_indicator(self, painter: QPainter) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Paint a small presence dot on bottom-right corner.\"\"\"\n        colors = {\n            \"online\": \"#4CAF50\",\n            \"busy\": \"#F44336\",\n            \"away\": \"#FFB300\",\n            \"offline\": \"#9E9E9E\",\n        }\n        radius = max(6, self.width() // 6)\n        center = QPoint(self.width() - radius, self.height() - radius)\n\n        painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\n        painter.setBrush(colors.get(self._presence, \"#9E9E9E\"))\n        painter.drawEllipse(center, radius, radius)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Context-menu actions\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _choose_avatar(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Open file-dialog for user to pick a new avatar picture.\"\"\"\n        filename, _ = QFileDialog.getOpenFileName(\n            self,\n            \"Select Avatar Picture\",\n            \"\",\n            \"Images (*.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.webp *.gif)\",\n        )\n        if filename:\n            self._handle_new_avatar(Path(filename))\n\n    def _handle_new_avatar(self, path: Path) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Validate and persist the chosen avatar (drag-drop or file-dialog).\"\"\"\n        if not path.exists():\n            return\n        if not path.suffix.lower() in {\".png\", \".jpg\", \".jpeg\", \".webp\", \".gif\"}:\n            QMessageBox.warning(self, \"Unsupported file\", \"File is not a supported image type.\")\n            return\n\n        self.set_avatar(str(path))\n        self.avatarChanged.emit(str(path))\n        # Publish to event-bus so other processes may sync the change\n        if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\n            self._event_bus.publish(  # type: ignore\n                \"avatar.changed\",\n                user_id=self._user_id,\n                path=str(path),\n            )\n\n    def _reveal_in_file_manager(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Platform-specific file manager reveal of the avatar file.\"\"\"\n        if not self._avatar_path:\n            return\n        try:\n            if sys.platform.startswith(\"darwin\"):\n                from subprocess import call\n\n                call([\"open\", \"--\", self._avatar_path])\n            elif os.name == \"nt\":\n                os.startfile(self._avatar_path)  # type: ignore  # pylint: disable=protected-access\n            else:  # linux, etc.\n                from subprocess import call\n\n                call([\"xdg-open\", self._avatar_path])\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            log.exception(\"Could not open file manager for %s\", self._avatar_path)\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/shared/widgets/avatar_widget.py",
    "numLines": 414,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 414
  },
  "type": "text"
}

21. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\ntests/integration/test_profile_sync.py\n\nEnd-to-end integration tests covering the profile-synchronisation layer that keeps\nuser preferences in-sync between micro-front-ends and the cloud profile service.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport contextlib\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional\n\nimport pytest\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                             \u2013\u2013\u2013 Test Doubles \u2013\u2013\u2013                            #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass NetworkError(ConnectionError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when the remote profile gateway is unreachable.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass FakeEventBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    *Extremely* lightweight pub/sub bus for integration testing.\n\n    It behaves like the in-process event bus the real application would use,\n    but is limited to fan-out broadcasts with no routing / filtering logic.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._subscribers: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\n\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Dispatch an event to all current subscribers.  Publishing is synchronous\n        but the individual consumers receive the events asynchronously via the\n        returned queue from :py:meth:`listen`.\n        \"\"\"\n        if topic not in self._subscribers:  # pragma: no cover\n            return  # No one cares.\n\n        for queue in self._subscribers[topic]:\n            # Use put_nowait so a slow consumer does *not* block the publisher.\n            queue.put_nowait(payload)\n\n    def listen(self, topic: str) -> \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to a topic and receive a dedicated queue that will carry all\n        future events for that topic.\n        \"\"\"\n        q: \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\" = asyncio.Queue()\n        self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, []).append(q)\n        return q\n\n\nclass FakeRemoteGateway:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-memory substitute of the cloud-backed profile service.\n\n    It emulates three main responsibilities:\n      1. Keeping the canonical *remote* profile for each user.\n      2. Detecting network availability and raising :class:`NetworkError`\n         accordingly.\n      3. Broadcasting updates from *any* client to *all* clients subscribed for\n         that user.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._remote_profiles: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\n        self._channels: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\n        self.offline: bool = False\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #                        \u2013\u2013\u2013 Public API (client) \u2013\u2013\u2013                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def push_profile(self, user_id: str, profile: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the profile to the 'cloud' and broadcast the change.\"\"\"\n        self._ensure_online()\n\n        # Use a *monotonic* timestamp as a version indicator (LWW conflict\n        # resolution).\n        merged = dict(profile)\n        merged[\"_last_modified\"] = time.monotonic()\n        self._remote_profiles[user_id] = merged\n\n        # Fan-out to listeners (non-blocking).\n        for q in self._channels.get(user_id, []):\n            q.put_nowait(merged)\n\n    def subscribe(self, user_id: str) -> \"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to server-side changes for *user_id* and receive a queue that\n        is fed whenever **another** client pushes a change.\n        \"\"\"\n        q: \"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\" = asyncio.Queue()\n        self._channels.setdefault(user_id, []).append(q)\n        return q\n\n    def snapshot(self, user_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Return the server-side profile for assertions.\"\"\"\n        return self._remote_profiles.get(user_id, {}).copy()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    #                           \u2013\u2013\u2013 Internals \u2013\u2013\u2013                        #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    def _ensure_online(self) -> None:\n        if self.offline:\n            raise NetworkError(\"Remote gateway is offline\")\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                       \u2013\u2013\u2013 System-Under-Test: Sync Service \u2013\u2013\u2013               #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass ProfileSyncService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Minimal, yet realistic, profile-synchronisation service.\n\n    A real implementation would be heavily event-driven, but for the purpose of\n    integration testing we drive the important mechanics manually.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    event_bus: FakeEventBus\n    gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n\n    local_profile: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    # Internal run-state\n    _unsynced_changes: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\n    _running: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\n    _remote_listener_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = field(default=None, init=False)\n\n    # ------------------------------- Life-Cycle ----------------------------- #\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Kick-off background tasks (remote listener).\"\"\"\n        if self._running:  # pragma: no cover\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._remote_listener_task = asyncio.create_task(self._listen_remote())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Gracefully shut down the background listener.\"\"\"\n        if not self._running:  # pragma: no cover\n            return\n\n        self._running = False\n        if self._remote_listener_task:\n            self._remote_listener_task.cancel()\n            with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):\n                await self._remote_listener_task\n\n    # ---------------------------- Public Interface ------------------------- #\n    def update_local(self, changes: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Called by the UI layer whenever the user modifies their preferences.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.local_profile.update(changes)\n        self._unsynced_changes = True\n\n        # Immediately broadcast to local components.\n        self.event_bus.publish(\n            f\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\", dict(self.local_profile)\n        )\n\n        # Fire-and-forget the sync attempt.\n        asyncio.create_task(self._attempt_sync())\n\n    async def on_network_restored(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        The connectivity monitor can poke this method once the network becomes\n        available again, so we can flush pending writes.\n        \"\"\"\n        await self._attempt_sync()\n\n    # ------------------------------- Internals ----------------------------- #\n    async def _attempt_sync(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Try to push local changes to the remote gateway.\"\"\"\n        if not self._unsynced_changes:\n            return\n\n        try:\n            await self.gateway.push_profile(self.user_id, dict(self.local_profile))\n            self._unsynced_changes = False\n        except NetworkError:\n            # Silently keep the dirty flag so we know to retry later.\n            pass\n\n    async def _listen_remote(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Background coroutine that receives server-side updates that originated\n        from *other* clients.\n        \"\"\"\n        q = self.gateway.subscribe(self.user_id)\n\n        while self._running:\n            remote_profile = await q.get()\n\n            # We only care when the incoming update carries a newer timestamp.\n            local_ts = self.local_profile.get(\"_last_modified\", -1)\n            remote_ts = remote_profile[\"_last_modified\"]\n            if remote_ts <= local_ts:\n                continue  # Local copy is newer or identical.\n\n            self.local_profile = dict(remote_profile)\n            self.event_bus.publish(\n                f\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\", dict(self.local_profile)\n            )\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                                \u2013\u2013\u2013 Helpers \u2013\u2013\u2013                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nasync def wait_for(\n    queue: \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\",\n    *,\n    predicate: Callable[[Any], bool] | None = None,\n    timeout: float = 2.0,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Utility that awaits until `predicate(item)` is *True* for an element read\n    from *queue*.  If *predicate* is :pydata:`None`, the call returns the first\n    element received.\n    \"\"\"\n    deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + timeout\n    predicate = predicate or (lambda _x: True)\n\n    while True:\n        remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()\n        if remaining <= 0:\n            raise TimeoutError(\"Timeout while waiting for queue event\")\n\n        item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=remaining)\n        if predicate(item):\n            return item\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                            \u2013\u2013\u2013 Integration Tests \u2013\u2013\u2013                        #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef event_bus() -> FakeEventBus:\n    return FakeEventBus()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef remote_gateway() -> FakeRemoteGateway:\n    return FakeRemoteGateway()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\nasync def services(event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway):\n    \"\"\"\n    Spin-up *two* sync services to simulate two independent micro-front-ends\n    running in different processes.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a = ProfileSyncService(\"alice\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\n    svc_b = ProfileSyncService(\"alice\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\n\n    await asyncio.gather(svc_a.start(), svc_b.start())\n    yield svc_a, svc_b\n\n    await asyncio.gather(svc_a.stop(), svc_b.stop())\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_local_update_propagates_to_remote_and_peers(\n    services, event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    When one service updates the local profile, the change should:\n\n      1. Appear on the remote gateway.\n      2. Be broadcast on the local event bus.\n      3. Update *other* micro-front-ends listening for that user.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a, svc_b = services\n    bus_q = event_bus.listen(\"profile.alice.updated\")\n\n    # Act \u2013 change a preference on service A.\n    svc_a.update_local({\"theme\": \"dark\"})\n\n    # 1) Wait for the bus broadcast and verify the payload.\n    msg = await wait_for(bus_q, predicate=lambda m: m.get(\"theme\") == \"dark\")\n    assert msg[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n\n    # 2) Eventually, remote profile should be in sync.\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Allow async push to complete.\n    assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n\n    # 3) The peer (service B) must have updated its local copy.\n    assert svc_b.local_profile[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_last_write_wins_conflict_resolution(\n    services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    When concurrent updates occur, the service implementing a *later* change\n    (higher `_last_modified`) must win the conflict.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a, svc_b = services\n\n    # Simulate near-simultaneous changes.\n    svc_a.update_local({\"language\": \"en\"})\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n    svc_b.update_local({\"language\": \"fr\"})  # <- later write wins.\n\n    # Give the sync loop a moment to converge.\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\n\n    snap = remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")\n    assert snap[\"language\"] == \"fr\"\n    assert svc_a.local_profile[\"language\"] == svc_b.local_profile[\"language\"] == \"fr\"\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_offline_changes_are_synced_when_network_recovers(\n    services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Offline edits must be queued locally and flushed to the server once\n    connectivity is restored.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a, _ = services\n\n    # Simulate loss of connectivity *before* the update.\n    remote_gateway.offline = True\n    svc_a.update_local({\"showAvatars\": False})\n\n    # Wait briefly to ensure the failed attempt has happened.\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n    assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\") == {}  # Nothing on the server.\n\n    # Bring the network back and trigger a retry.\n    remote_gateway.offline = False\n    await svc_a.on_network_restored()\n\n    # The push should succeed shortly after.\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n    assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")[\"showAvatars\"] is False\n    assert svc_a._unsynced_changes is False\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py",
    "numLines": 348,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 348
  },
  "type": "text"
}

23. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.main\n================================\n\nReal-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\nsubscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\na reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\nimplemented with an MVVM flavour.\n\nThe file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\nFlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\n\nCopyright:\n    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport logging\nimport pkg_resources\nimport signal\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (\n    QApplication,\n    QLabel,\n    QListWidget,\n    QListWidgetItem,\n    QVBoxLayout,\n    QWidget,\n)\n\nLOG = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.presence\")\nLOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Event-Bus Abstractions\n################################################################################\nclass EventBus(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\"\"\"\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass _InProcessBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\n    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\n    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Publish: %s -> %s\", topic, payload)\n        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                await handler(payload)\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                LOG.exception(\"Handler error for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\n        LOG.debug(\"Subscribed %s to %s\", handler, topic)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Model\n################################################################################\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass UserPresence:\n    \"\"\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: str  # e.g. \"online\", \"away\", \"dnd\", \"offline\"\n    last_active: datetime\n    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def is_online(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\"\"\"\n        if self.status in {\"offline\", \"unknown\"}:\n            return False\n        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\n        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# View-Model\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\n    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presence_changed = Signal()\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\n    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\n        return sorted(\n            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\n        )\n\n    @Slot(dict)\n    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\n        emits a notification to the UI controls.\n        \"\"\"\n        user = UserPresence(\n            user_id=evt[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=evt.get(\"display_name\", evt[\"user_id\"]),\n            status=evt.get(\"status\", \"unknown\"),\n            last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt[\"last_active\"]),\n            avatar_url=evt.get(\"avatar_url\"),\n        )\n        LOG.debug(\"Presence updated: %s\", user)\n        self._users[user.user_id] = user\n        self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\"\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Qt View\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\n    It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\n    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._setup_ui()\n        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\n    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\n        self.setWindowTitle(\"Presence\")\n        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n        self._list = QListWidget(self)\n        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n        layout.addWidget(self._list)\n\n    @Slot()\n    def _refresh(self) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Refresh UI\")\n        self._list.clear()\n        for user in self._vm.all_users():\n            item = QListWidgetItem()\n            prefix = \"\ud83d\udfe2\" if user.is_online() else \"\u26ab\"\n            item.setText(f\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\")\n            if user.avatar_url:\n                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\n                if not pixmap.isNull():\n                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\n            self._list.addItem(item)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Presence Service (async)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Background asyncio component responsible for:\n\n        \u2022 Subscribing to the presence topic\n        \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _TOPIC = \"users.presence\"\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._qt_obj = qt_obj\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users\n        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\n\n        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)\n        await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\n        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\n    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\n        thread where the view-model lives.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\n        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\n        LOG.debug(\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\", payload)\n        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self._qt_obj,\n            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\n            Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": self._local_user(),\n            \"display_name\": self._local_user(),  # demo\n            \"status\": status,\n            \"last_active\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"avatar_url\": None,\n        }\n        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\n\n    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(30)\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\n    @staticmethod\n    def _local_user() -> str:\n        # In real life we would ask the identity service\n        return Path.home().name\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceProvider(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\n    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\n    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\n    entry-point.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n\n\ndef _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\n    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\n    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\"flockdesk_presence_provider\"):\n        try:\n            factory = entry_point.load()\n            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n            LOG.info(\"Loaded presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to load presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n    return providers\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Bootstrap\n################################################################################\nasync def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:\n    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\n\n    vm = PresenceViewModel()\n    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\n    widget.resize(240, 400)\n    widget.show()\n\n    # Async service & plugins\n    service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)\n    await service.start()\n\n    for prov in _load_providers():\n        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\n    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\n    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n    qtimer = QTimer()\n    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\n    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\n    qtimer.start()\n\n    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\n\n    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\n    executed directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\",\n    )\n    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        LOG.info(\"Shutting down.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    main()\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py",
    "numLines": 337,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 337
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nFlockDesk \u2013 Social Workspace Orchestrator\n=========================================\ncore/main.py\n\nThis module is the primary entry-point for the entire desktop suite.  It is\nresponsible for:\n\n1.  Boot-strapping a Qt application with sane defaults.\n2.  Wiring up the internal asyncio-powered event-bus.\n3.  Loading, validating and life-cycling runtime plugins.\n4.  Registering global diagnostics (loggers, Sentry, unhandled-exception hooks).\n5.  Spawning the root window and handing execution over to the Qt event-loop.\n\nThe code is intentionally separated from any concrete UI widgets;  micro-front-\nends are loaded at runtime as plugins.  This keeps the core slim, testable and\nagnostic of any particular feature.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport inspect\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport traceback\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom functools import wraps\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom types import ModuleType\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Coroutine, Dict, List, Optional, Set\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Optional runtime dependencies\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\ntry:\n    import sentry_sdk\nexcept ImportError:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 soft dependency\n    sentry_sdk = None  # type: ignore\n\ntry:\n    from PySide6.QtCore import Qt, QObject, Signal, Slot, QTimer  # noqa\n    from PySide6.QtGui import QIcon  # noqa\n    from PySide6.QtWidgets import QApplication, QMainWindow, QMessageBox  # noqa\nexcept ImportError:  # pragma: no cover\n    raise RuntimeError(\n        \"PySide6 is not installed.  Install with `pip install PySide6`.\"\n    ) from None\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Globals & Constants\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nAPP_NAME = \"FlockDesk\"\nORG_NAME = \"Flockware Inc.\"\nCFG_DIR = Path(os.getenv(\"XDG_CONFIG_HOME\", Path.home() / \".config\")) / \"flockdesk\"\nPLUGIN_DIR = Path.home() / \".flockdesk\" / \"plugins\"\nDEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE = CFG_DIR / \"settings.json\"\nSENTRY_DSN_ENV = \"FLOCKDESK_SENTRY_DSN\"\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.core\")\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Utility wrappers\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef async_slot(func: Callable[..., Awaitable[Any]]) -> Callable:\n    \"\"\"\n    Decorator to make an `async def` Qt slot look synchronous.\n\n    Qt does not understand `async def` methods directly.  Wrapping the coroutine\n    ensures any exceptions are captured and re-routed to the central handler.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @Slot()\n    @wraps(func)\n    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\n        loop.create_task(func(*args, **kwargs))\n\n    return wrapper\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Runtime configuration\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RuntimeConfig:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-memory representation of user / workspace configuration.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    theme: str = \"dark\"\n    autoupdate: bool = True\n    plugin_search_paths: List[str] = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: [str(PLUGIN_DIR.resolve())]\n    )\n    sentry_dsn: Optional[str] = None\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load(cls, path: Path = DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE) -> \"RuntimeConfig\":\n        if not path.exists():\n            _log.warning(\"No config file found at %s \u2013 falling back to defaults.\", path)\n            return cls()\n        try:\n            with path.open(\"r\") as fp:\n                data = json.load(fp)\n            return cls(**data)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            _log.exception(\"Failed to parse configuration \u2013 using defaults.\")\n            return cls()\n\n    def save(self, path: Path = DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE) -> None:\n        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        with path.open(\"w\") as fp:\n            json.dump(self.__dict__, fp, indent=2)\n        _log.debug(\"Configuration persisted to %s\", path)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Event bus\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\nclass Event:\n    \"\"\"\n    Simple event container.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = (\"type\", \"payload\", \"origin\")\n\n    def __init__(self, type: str, payload: Any = None, origin: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.type = type\n        self.payload = payload\n        self.origin = origin\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"Event(type={self.type!r}, origin={self.origin!r}, payload={self.payload!r})\"\n\n\nclass EventBus(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cross-process global event-bus.\n\n    Internally implemented with:\n    \u2022 A Qt `Signal` for UI thread-safe broadcasting.\n    \u2022 A singleton asyncio event-loop for async subscribers.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    qt_broadcast = Signal(object)  # Event\n\n    def __init__(self, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop):\n        super().__init__()\n        self._loop = loop\n        self._subscribers: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Event], Coroutine | None]]] = {}\n\n        # Connect Qt \u2192 async\n        self.qt_broadcast.connect(self._dispatch_qt)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self, event_type: str, callback: Callable[[Event], Awaitable | None]\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Register a callback (async or sync) for a given event type.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._subscribers.setdefault(event_type, []).append(callback)\n\n    def publish(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Publish an event globally \u2013 thread safe via Qt signal.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.qt_broadcast.emit(event)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @Slot(object)\n    def _dispatch_qt(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fan out an event to registered callbacks.\n        \"\"\"\n        for callback in self._subscribers.get(event.type, []):\n            result = callback(event)\n            if inspect.iscoroutine(result):\n                self._loop.create_task(result)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Plugin manager\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PluginMeta:\n    \"\"\"\n    Metadata extracted from the plugin module.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    name: str\n    version: str\n    module: ModuleType\n    provides: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set)\n\n\nclass PluginManager:\n    \"\"\"\n    Discovers and loads plugins from a configurable search path.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    MANIFEST_ATTR = \"FD_PLUGIN_MANIFEST\"\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, cfg: RuntimeConfig):\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._cfg = cfg\n        self._loaded: Dict[str, PluginMeta] = {}\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Discovery\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def discover(self) -> List[Path]:\n        manifests: List[Path] = []\n        for raw_dir in self._cfg.plugin_search_paths:\n            path = Path(raw_dir).expanduser()\n            if not path.exists():\n                _log.debug(\"Plugin path %s does not exist \u2013 skipping.\", path)\n                continue\n            manifests.extend(path.glob(\"*/__init__.py\"))\n        _log.info(\"Discovered %d potential plugins.\", len(manifests))\n        return manifests\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Loading\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def load_all(self) -> None:\n        for manifest in self.discover():\n            try:\n                meta = self._load_plugin_module(manifest)\n                self._loaded[meta.name] = meta\n                _log.info(\"Loaded plugin %s v%s\", meta.name, meta.version)\n            except Exception:\n                _log.error(\"Failed to load plugin at %s\", manifest)\n                _log.debug(traceback.format_exc())\n\n    def _load_plugin_module(self, manifest: Path) -> PluginMeta:\n        package_root = manifest.parent\n        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n            package_root.name, manifest, submodule_search_locations=[str(package_root)]\n        )\n        if spec is None or spec.loader is None:  # pragma: no cover\n            raise ImportError(f\"Cannot load plugin from {manifest}\")\n\n        module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n        sys.modules[spec.name] = module\n        spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        manifest_dict = getattr(module, self.MANIFEST_ATTR, None)\n        if manifest_dict is None:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Plugin {module.__name__} does not expose {self.MANIFEST_ATTR}\"\n            )\n\n        meta = PluginMeta(\n            name=manifest_dict[\"name\"],\n            version=manifest_dict.get(\"version\", \"0.0.0\"),\n            module=module,\n            provides=set(manifest_dict.get(\"provides\", [])),\n        )\n\n        # Call plugin initialiser if present\n        init_callable = getattr(module, \"setup\", None)\n        if callable(init_callable):\n            init_callable(bus=self._bus, config=self._cfg)\n        return meta\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Crash handling / diagnostics\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef _init_logging(verbose: bool = False) -> None:\n    handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream=sys.stdout)\n    fmt = \"[%(asctime)s] %(levelname)s %(name)s \u2013 %(message)s\"\n    handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter(fmt))\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.DEBUG if verbose else logging.INFO, handlers=[handler]\n    )\n\n\ndef _init_sentry(dsn: Optional[str]) -> None:\n    if not dsn:\n        _log.debug(\"Sentry disabled \u2013 no DSN provided.\")\n        return\n\n    if sentry_sdk is None:\n        _log.warning(\"sentry-sdk not installed;  skipping error reporting.\")\n        return\n\n    sentry_sdk.init(\n        dsn=dsn,\n        traces_sample_rate=0.1,\n        release=f\"{APP_NAME}@{_read_version()}\",\n    )\n    _log.info(\"Sentry initialised.\")\n\n\ndef _read_version() -> str:\n    try:\n        from importlib.metadata import version\n\n        return version(\"flockdesk\")\n    except Exception:\n        return \"0.0.0\"\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Qt Application bootstrap\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\nclass MainWindow(QMainWindow):\n    \"\"\"\n    Placeholder root window.  Real UI components are contributed via plugins.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, cfg: RuntimeConfig, plugin_manager: PluginManager):\n        super().__init__()\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._cfg = cfg\n        self._plugin_manager = plugin_manager\n        self._init_ui()\n        self._wire_events()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Setup\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _init_ui(self) -> None:\n        self.setWindowTitle(APP_NAME)\n        self.resize(1200, 800)\n        icon_path = Path(__file__).with_name(\"logo.png\")\n        if icon_path.exists():\n            self.setWindowIcon(QIcon(str(icon_path)))\n\n    def _wire_events(self) -> None:\n        # Example: listen for global notifications\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"global.notification\", self._on_global_notification)\n\n    async def _on_global_notification(self, ev: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Display passive notification.\n        \"\"\"\n        QMessageBox.information(self, \"Notification\", str(ev.payload))\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Application life-cycle\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Application:\n    \"\"\"\n    The orchestrator that drives Qt <-> asyncio cooperation.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    argv: List[str]\n    cfg: RuntimeConfig = field(default_factory=RuntimeConfig)\n    loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: asyncio.new_event_loop()\n    )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Boot\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Main entrypoint.  Does not return until application exit.\n        \"\"\"\n        asyncio.set_event_loop(self.loop)\n        self._parse_cli()\n\n        _init_logging(verbose=os.getenv(\"FLOCKDESK_DEBUG\", \"\").lower() in (\"1\", \"true\"))\n        _init_sentry(self.cfg.sentry_dsn or os.getenv(SENTRY_DSN_ENV))\n\n        # Qt must run in main thread\n        app = QApplication(self.argv)\n        app.setApplicationName(APP_NAME)\n        app.setOrganizationName(ORG_NAME)\n\n        # Prepare core services\n        bus = EventBus(self.loop)\n        plugin_manager = PluginManager(bus, self.cfg)\n\n        # Load plugins in background to avoid UI freeze\n        self.loop.create_task(self._load_plugins_async(plugin_manager))\n\n        window = MainWindow(bus, self.cfg, plugin_manager)\n        window.show()\n\n        # Let Qt drive the asyncio loop\n        timer = QTimer()\n        timer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)\n        timer.start(20)\n\n        # Integrate loops\n        self.loop.call_soon(lambda: _log.debug(\"Event loop started.\"))\n        with self._enter_exception_handler():\n            self.loop.run_until_complete(self._qt_event_loop(app))\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def _qt_event_loop(self, qt_app: QApplication) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run Qt event loop inside asyncio until Qt exits.\n        \"\"\"\n        await asyncio.get_running_loop().run_in_executor(None, qt_app.exec)\n\n    async def _load_plugins_async(self, pm: PluginManager) -> None:\n        await asyncio.sleep(0)  # yield to Qt render\n        pm.load_all()\n        _log.info(\"All plugins loaded (%d).\", len(pm._loaded))\n\n    def _parse_cli(self) -> None:\n        parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(APP_NAME)\n        parser.add_argument(\n            \"--config\",\n            type=Path,\n            help=\"Override configuration file (JSON).\",\n        )\n        parser.add_argument(\n            \"--disable-autoupdate\",\n            action=\"store_true\",\n            help=\"Disable automatic update checks.\",\n        )\n        args, unknown = parser.parse_known_args(self.argv[1:])\n\n        if args.config:\n            self.cfg = RuntimeConfig.load(args.config)\n\n        if args.disable_autoupdate:\n            self.cfg.autoupdate = False\n\n        if unknown:\n            _log.debug(\"Ignored unknown CLI args: %s\", unknown)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Context managers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    from contextlib import contextmanager\n\n    @contextmanager\n    def _enter_exception_handler(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Wrap event-loop execution to catch unhandled exceptions,\n        send them to Sentry, and display a user-friendly message.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            yield\n        except Exception as exc:\n            _log.critical(\"Unhandled exception: %s\", exc, exc_info=exc)\n            if sentry_sdk:\n                sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)\n            QMessageBox.critical(\n                None,\n                f\"{APP_NAME} crashed\",\n                \"Uh-oh!  Something went terribly wrong.  \"\n                \"A crash report has been sent, and we're sorry for the inconvenience.\",\n            )\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Main script entrypoint\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # noqa\n    Application(sys.argv).run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/flockdesk/core/main.py",
    "numLines": 503,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 503
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "command": "cd /app/project && for f in $(find . -name \"*.py\"); do\n  first_line=$(sed -n '1p' \"$f\")\n  if [ \"$first_line\" = '```python' ]; then\n    echo \"Fixing: $f\"\n    # Remove first line (```python) and last line (```)\n    sed -i '1d;$d' \"$f\"\n  fi\ndone",
  "description": "Fix all Python files by removing markdown code fences"
}

{
  "command": "cd /app/project && python3 << 'EOF'\nimport os\nimport glob\n\nfor f in glob.glob(\"**/*.py\", recursive=True):\n    with open(f, 'r') as file:\n        content = file.read()\n    \n    # Check if the file starts with ```python\n    if content.startswith('```python'):\n        # Remove the first line (```python) and the last line (```)\n        lines = content.split('\\n')\n        if lines[0] == '```python' and lines[-1] == '```':\n            lines = lines[1:-1]  # Remove first and last\n        elif lines[0] == '```python':\n            lines = lines[1:]  # Just remove first\n        new_content = '\\n'.join(lines)\n        with open(f, 'w') as file:\n            file.write(new_content)\n        print(f\"Fixed: {f}\")\n\nprint(\"Done!\")\nEOF\n",
  "description": "Fix all Python files using Python script"
}

32. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/tests/conftest.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\ntests/conftest.py\n\nShared pytest configuration & fixtures for the FlockDesk desktop suite.\n\nThe fixtures below try to isolate the *real* application stack as much as\npossible while still giving tests full control over:\n\n1.  A temporary, writable configuration/profile directory that is injected\n    through the same environment variables the application uses in production.\n2.  A lightweight in-memory event-bus implementation so that individual\n    services can be unit-tested without boot-strapping the full IPC layer.\n3.  An optional Qt application instance, created only when tests explicitly\n    ask for it (\u2012k 'qt' or via the `requires_qt` marker).  The fixture is\n    skipped automatically when PySide6/Qt is not available \u2013 this allows the\n    test-suite to run in headless CI environments without a full Qt tool-chain.\n4.  A dummy plugin-registry that mimics the public interface expected by the\n    production PluginManager.  It lets tests register arbitrary callables/\n    classes as \u201cplugins\u201d and provides helpers for assertions.\n\nPytest hooks:\n    * pytest_addoption\n    * pytest_configure\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport contextlib\nimport inspect\nimport os\nimport shutil\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom types import ModuleType\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Iterable, List, Mapping, MutableMapping\n\nimport pytest\n\n###############################################################################\n# \u2013\u2013\u2013 Optional Qt imports \u2013\u2013\u2013\n###############################################################################\n\ntry:\n    # PySide6 is preferred, but PyQt6 is accepted as a fallback.\n    from PySide6 import QtWidgets  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:  # pragma: no cover\n    try:\n        from PyQt6 import QtWidgets  # type: ignore\n    except ModuleNotFoundError:  # pragma: no cover\n        QtWidgets = None  # type: ignore\n\n\n###############################################################################\n# \u2013\u2013\u2013 CLI options & markers \u2013\u2013\u2013\n###############################################################################\n\n\ndef pytest_addoption(parser: pytest.Parser) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Adds command-line options to enable/disable heavy integration layers during CI.\n    \"\"\"\n    group = parser.getgroup(\"flockdesk\")\n    group.addoption(\n        \"--run-gui\",\n        action=\"store_true\",\n        default=False,\n        help=\"Run tests that require a Qt GUI event-loop.\",\n    )\n\n\ndef pytest_configure(config: pytest.Config) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Register custom markers so that `pytest --markers` shows meaningful docs.\n    \"\"\"\n    config.addinivalue_line(\n        \"markers\",\n        \"requires_qt: mark test as needing a Qt GUI (skipped when Qt is missing \"\n        \"or when --run-gui is not specified).\",\n    )\n\n\n###############################################################################\n# \u2013\u2013\u2013 Helper classes \u2013\u2013\u2013\n###############################################################################\n\n\nclass InMemoryEventBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Minimal thread-safe event-bus for unit-tests.  The public surface mimics the\n    *real* production interface just enough to fill in when the heavy IPC layer\n    isn't spun up.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._subscribers: MutableMapping[str, List[Callable[[Any], None]]] = (\n            defaultdict(list)\n        )\n        self._loop = asyncio.get_event_loop_policy().get_event_loop()\n        self._closed = False\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #  Subscription                                                          #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def subscribe(self, topic: str, handler: Callable[[Any], None]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Register *handler* to be invoked synchronously when *topic* is\n        published.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._closed:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot subscribe on a closed event-bus.\")\n        if not callable(handler):\n            raise TypeError(\"Handler must be callable.\")\n\n        self._subscribers[topic].append(handler)\n\n    def unsubscribe(self, topic: str, handler: Callable[[Any], None]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Remove *handler* from *topic* subscription list.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            self._subscribers[topic].remove(handler)\n        except (KeyError, ValueError):\n            # Silently ignore un-subscriptions that do not exist to make test\n            # clean-up easier.\n            pass\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #  Publish                                                               #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any | None = None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast *payload* to all handlers subscribed to *topic*.  The\n        broadcast is synchronous but automatically executed in the current\n        asyncio event-loop if an async def handler is detected.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._closed:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot publish on a closed event-bus.\")\n\n        for handler in list(self._subscribers.get(topic, ())):\n            if inspect.iscoroutinefunction(handler):\n                self._loop.create_task(handler(payload))\n            else:\n                handler(payload)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #  House-keeping                                                         #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prevent further publish/subscribe operations.  Intended to be called\n        from fixture teardown.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._subscribers.clear()\n        self._closed = True\n\n\nclass DummyPluginRegistry:\n    \"\"\"\n    Replace the heavy-weight plugin-manager with a feather-weight registry that\n    can register arbitrary callables/classes for unit-testing.\n\n    Example usage in a test:\n\n    def test_my_service(plugin_registry):\n        @plugin_registry.register('service')\n        class MyService:\n            ...\n\n        plugin = plugin_registry.get('service')\n        assert isinstance(plugin, MyService)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._plugins: Dict[str, Any] = {}\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    #  API expected by production code                                   #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def register(self, name: str) -> Callable[[Any], Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Decorator that registers a class/function as a plugin under *name*.\n\n            @plugin_registry.register(\"polls\")\n            class PollsPlugin: ...\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def decorator(symbol: Any) -> Any:\n            self._plugins[name] = symbol\n            return symbol\n\n        return decorator\n\n    def get(self, name: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Retrieve the plugin registered under *name*.  Raises KeyError when not\n        found \u2013 mirrors production behaviour.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._plugins[name]\n\n    def all(self) -> Mapping[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Read-only mapping of all registered plugins.\n        \"\"\"\n        return dict(self._plugins)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    #  Dunder hooks to ease assertions                                   #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def __contains__(self, item: str) -> bool:  # pragma: no cover\n        return item in self._plugins\n\n    def __iter__(self) -> Iterable[str]:  # pragma: no cover\n        return iter(self._plugins)\n\n    def __len__(self) -> int:  # pragma: no cover\n        return len(self._plugins)\n\n\n###############################################################################\n# \u2013\u2013\u2013 Fixtures \u2013\u2013\u2013\n###############################################################################\n\n\n@pytest.fixture(scope=\"session\")\ndef event_loop(request: pytest.FixtureRequest) -> asyncio.AbstractEventLoop:  # noqa: D401\n    \"\"\"\n    A session-wide asyncio event-loop.  Needed for fixtures/tests that run\n    asynchronous code without spawning their own loop.\n    \"\"\"\n    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\n    yield loop\n    loop.call_soon(loop.stop)\n    with contextlib.suppress(RuntimeError):\n        loop.close()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture(scope=\"session\")\ndef tmp_user_profile_dir() -> Path:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a temporary directory that pretends to be the user\u2019s roaming profile.\n    The real application uses the *FLOCKDESK_PROFILE_DIR* environment variable\n    to locate user settings, layouts, caches, etc.\n    \"\"\"\n    tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix=\"flockdesk-profile-\"))\n    os.environ[\"FLOCKDESK_PROFILE_DIR\"] = str(tmp_dir)\n    yield tmp_dir\n    # Safety-net: Remove the dir recursively in teardown.\n    shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir, ignore_errors=True)\n    os.environ.pop(\"FLOCKDESK_PROFILE_DIR\", None)\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef event_bus() -> InMemoryEventBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Provide a fresh in-memory event-bus for each test function.\n    \"\"\"\n    bus = InMemoryEventBus()\n    yield bus\n    bus.close()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef plugin_registry() -> DummyPluginRegistry:\n    \"\"\"\n    Provide an isolated plugin registry for a test.  This avoids cross-test\n    contamination when plugins are registered via decorators.\n    \"\"\"\n    return DummyPluginRegistry()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef qapp(request: pytest.FixtureRequest) -> QtWidgets.QApplication:  # type: ignore\n    \"\"\"\n    Lazily start a single QApplication for tests that are explicitly marked with\n    ``@pytest.mark.requires_qt`` or when ``--run-gui`` is passed.\n\n    The fixture automatically skips the test when:\n        * Qt bindings are not importable\n        * The user did not opt-in via --run-gui (to keep headless default)\n    \"\"\"\n    if not request.config.getoption(\"--run-gui\"):\n        pytest.skip(\"--run-gui not supplied; skipping Qt tests.\")\n\n    if QtWidgets is None:  # pragma: no cover\n        pytest.skip(\"Qt bindings not available in this environment.\")\n\n    # QApplication must be instantiated only once per process.  We re-use the\n    # global instance if it already exists.\n    app = QtWidgets.QApplication.instance() or QtWidgets.QApplication(sys.argv)\n\n    yield app\n\n    # Tear-down: flush any queued events so that background timers/timeouts do\n    # not leak into other tests.\n    with contextlib.suppress(Exception):\n        app.processEvents()\n```\n",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/conftest.py",
    "numLines": 307,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 307
  },
  "type": "text"
}
