44. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/tmp/project_copy/flockdesk/core/app.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\"\"\"\nflockdesk.core.app\n==================\n\nThe central orchestration runtime for the FlockDesk desktop suite.\nResponsible for:\n\n* Application-wide lifecycle & graceful shutdown\n* Global event-bus for intra-service communication\n* Plugin discovery, dependency wiring and hot-reload hooks\n* Persisted user settings & dynamic preference updates\n* Crash-reporting funnel to Sentry ( sandboxed per-service )\n* Asyncio <-> Qt/GUI message-pump coherency\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport importlib.util\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport signal\nimport sys\nimport traceback\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom types import ModuleType\nfrom typing import (\n    Any,\n    Awaitable,\n    Callable,\n    Coroutine,\n    Dict,\n    Iterable,\n    List,\n    Optional,\n    Protocol,\n    Set,\n    Tuple,\n    Union,\n)\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Optional / soft dependencies                                               #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ntry:\n    from PySide6.QtCore import QCoreApplication, QObject, QTimer  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:  # Headless / non-GUI environments\n    QCoreApplication = None  # type: ignore\n    QObject = object  # type: ignore\n    QTimer = None  # type: ignore\n\ntry:\n    import appdirs  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:\n    appdirs = None\n\ntry:\n    import sentry_sdk  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:\n    sentry_sdk = None  # noqa: N816\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Logging setup                                                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.core\")\nlogger.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n_handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream=sys.stdout)\n_handler.setFormatter(\n    logging.Formatter(\"%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s\")\n)\nlogger.addHandler(_handler)\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Typing                                                                     #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nJSON_T = Union[str, int, float, bool, None, Dict[str, \"JSON_T\"], List[\"JSON_T\"]]\n\n\nclass Event:\n    \"\"\"\n    A lightweight event envelope exchanged over the FlockDesk event bus.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = (\"topic\", \"payload\", \"meta\")\n\n    def __init__(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\n        self.topic: str = topic\n        self.payload: Any = payload\n        self.meta: Dict[str, Any] = meta\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"Event({self.topic!r}, payload={self.payload!r}, meta={self.meta!r})\"\n\n\nListener = Callable[[Event], Union[None, Awaitable[None]]]\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Event Bus Implementation                                                   #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass EventBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    A minimal in-process publish/subscribe mechanism.\n\n    The bus is intentionally *na\u00efve* but good enough for inter-plugin traffic.\n    More advanced requirements (wildcards, QoS, type routing) can be layered\n    on top by dedicated broker services.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._subscribers: Dict[str, Set[Listener]] = {}\n        self._loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Subscription                                                          #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def subscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\n        listeners = self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, set())\n        listeners.add(listener)\n        logger.debug(\"Listener %s subscribed to topic %s\", listener, topic)\n\n    def unsubscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\n        try:\n            self._subscribers[topic].discard(listener)\n            logger.debug(\"Listener %s unsubscribed from topic %s\", listener, topic)\n        except KeyError:\n            logger.warning(\"Attempted to unsubscribe unknown topic %s\", topic)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Publishing                                                            #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Schedule the dispatch of an event to all interested listeners.\n        \"\"\"\n        event = Event(topic, payload, **meta)\n        logger.debug(\"Publishing event %s\", event)\n        listeners = self._subscribers.get(topic, set()).copy()\n\n        for listener in listeners:\n            try:\n                result = listener(event)\n                if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):\n                    # Fire-and-forget, but capture exceptions.\n                    task = self._loop.create_task(result)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n                    task.add_done_callback(self._capture_async_exception)\n            except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\n                    \"Synchronous listener %s crashed while handling %s: %s\",\n                    listener,\n                    topic,\n                    exc,\n                )\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Helpers                                                               #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _capture_async_exception(self, task: asyncio.Task[Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"\n        Funnel unhandled exceptions from background tasks into the logger.\n        \"\"\"\n        if task.cancelled():\n            return\n        if exc := task.exception():\n            logger.exception(\"Async listener raised: %s\", exc)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Plugin System                                                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass Plugin(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Public interface that all plugins must comply with.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    name: str\n    version: str\n\n    def setup(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n    async def start(self) -> None: ...\n    async def shutdown(self) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass PluginLoader:\n    \"\"\"\n    Discover & instantiate plugins.\n\n    *User* plugins are loaded from the following search order:\n\n    1. `$FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS` (semicolon separated list of paths)\n    2. `~/.flockdesk/plugins`\n    3. `<install-root>/flockdesk/plugins`\n    \"\"\"\n\n    SEARCH_LOCATIONS: Tuple[Path, ...] = (\n        Path(os.getenv(\"FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS\", \"\")),\n        Path.home() / \".flockdesk\" / \"plugins\",\n        Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"plugins\",\n    )\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._plugins: List[Plugin] = []\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Discovery                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def discover(self) -> List[Plugin]:\n        logger.info(\"Discovering plugins \u2026\")\n        for location in self.SEARCH_LOCATIONS:\n            if not location or not location.exists():\n                continue\n            for path in location.glob(\"*/__init__.py\"):\n                try:\n                    plugin = self._load_module(path)\n                    self._plugins.append(plugin)\n                    logger.info(\"Loaded plugin: %s %s\", plugin.name, plugin.version)\n                except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                    logger.error(\n                        \"Failed to load plugin at %s:\\n%s\",\n                        path,\n                        traceback.format_exc(),\n                    )\n        return self._plugins\n\n    def _load_module(self, init_file: Path) -> Plugin:\n        module_name = f\"flockdesk.plugin.{init_file.parent.name}\"\n        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, init_file)\n        if not spec or not spec.loader:\n            raise ImportError(f\"Cannot build spec for {init_file}\")\n        module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n        sys.modules[module_name] = module\n        spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        if not hasattr(module, \"Plugin\"):\n            raise AttributeError(\n                f\"{module_name} does not expose a 'Plugin' class\"\n            )  # noqa: TRY003\n\n        plugin_cls = getattr(module, \"Plugin\")\n        plugin: Plugin = plugin_cls()  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n\n        plugin.setup(self._bus)\n        return plugin\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Lifecycle                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def start_all(self) -> None:\n        for plugin in self._plugins:\n            try:\n                await plugin.start()\n                logger.info(\"Plugin started: %s\", plugin.name)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to start\", plugin.name)\n\n    async def shutdown_all(self) -> None:\n        for plugin in reversed(self._plugins):\n            try:\n                await plugin.shutdown()\n                logger.info(\"Plugin stopped: %s\", plugin.name)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to shutdown\", plugin.name)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Settings / Configuration                                                   #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass SettingsManager:\n    \"\"\"\n    Persisted key/value store backed by a JSON blob in the user's config dir.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    FILE_NAME = \"settings.json\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._data: Dict[str, JSON_T] = {}\n        self._path = self._resolve_path()\n        self._load()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API                                                            #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def get(self, key: str, default: JSON_T | None = None) -> JSON_T | None:\n        return self._data.get(key, default)\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: JSON_T) -> None:\n        self._data[key] = value\n        self._save_async()\n\n    def all(self) -> Dict[str, JSON_T]:\n        return dict(self._data)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Persistence                                                           #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _load(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            if self._path.exists():\n                self._data = json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n                logger.debug(\"Settings loaded from %s\", self._path)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:\n            logger.warning(\"Failed to load settings (%s), starting fresh\", exc)\n            self._data = {}\n\n    def _save_async(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Off-load writing to disk to avoid blocking the GUI / event-loop.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        async def _writer() -> None:\n            try:\n                self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n                self._path.write_text(json.dumps(self._data, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n                logger.debug(\"Settings persisted to %s\", self._path)\n            except OSError as exc:\n                logger.error(\"Could not persist settings: %s\", exc)\n\n        asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_writer())\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Helpers                                                               #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    @staticmethod\n    def _resolve_path() -> Path:\n        if appdirs:\n            cfg_dir = Path(appdirs.user_config_dir(\"FlockDesk\", \"Flockware\"))\n        else:\n            cfg_dir = Path.home() / \".config\" / \"flockdesk\"\n        return cfg_dir / SettingsManager.FILE_NAME\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Crash / Diagnostics                                                        #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass CrashReporter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Thin wrapper around Sentry so we can easily replace/disable later on.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\n        self._enabled: bool = False\n        if dsn and sentry_sdk:\n            sentry_sdk.init(dsn=dsn, release=\"flockdesk@1.0.0\")\n            self._enabled = True\n            logger.info(\"Crash reporting enabled\")\n        elif dsn:\n            logger.warning(\"sentry-sdk not installed, crash reporting disabled\")\n\n    async def capture_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:\n        if self._enabled:\n            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Core Application                                                           #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass FlockDeskCoreApp(QObject if QObject is not object else object):  # type: ignore[misc]\n    \"\"\"\n    Central bootstrap for FlockDesk.\n\n    Spins up the event-bus, plugin runtime, setting manager, and bridges\n    between the asyncio reactor and Qt's message-pump if available.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, *, headless: bool = False) -> None:\n        if not headless and QCoreApplication is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"PySide6 not installed \u2011 cannot run in GUI mode. \"\n                \"Pass `headless=True` to run without Qt.\"\n            )\n\n        super().__init__()  # type: ignore[misc]\n        self._headless = headless\n        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\n        asyncio.set_event_loop(self._loop)\n\n        self.bus = EventBus()\n        self.settings = SettingsManager()\n        self.crash = CrashReporter(\n            dsn=self.settings.get(\"sentry_dsn\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n        )\n        self._plugin_loader = PluginLoader(self.bus)\n\n        self._closing = asyncio.Event()\n\n        # Connect SIGINT / SIGTERM\n        for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n            signal.signal(sig, self._signal_handler)\n\n        if not self._headless:\n            self._qt_app: QCoreApplication = QCoreApplication(sys.argv)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            # Pump asyncio via Qt's event loop every 15ms\n            QTimer.singleShot(0, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Bootstrap                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Synchronous entry-point; block until shutdown completes.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Starting FlockDesk \u2026\")\n        try:\n            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._async_main())\n        finally:\n            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._loop.shutdown_asyncgens())\n            self._loop.close()\n            logger.info(\"FlockDesk stopped\")\n\n    async def _async_main(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Coroutine that manages the asynchronous lifecycle.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Plugin discovery/startup\n        self._plugin_loader.discover()\n        await self._plugin_loader.start_all()\n\n        # Run until a shutdown signal is received\n        await self._closing.wait()\n\n        # Tear-down\n        await self._plugin_loader.shutdown_all()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Shutdown & Signals                                                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _signal_handler(self, signum: int, _frame: Any | None = None) -> None:\n        logger.info(\"Received signal %s, shutting down \u2026\", signum)\n        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._closing.set)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Qt / Async Integration                                                #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _qt_asyncio_integration_tick(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute all asyncio tasks waiting on the loop & re-schedule next tick.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            self._loop.call_soon(self._loop.stop)\n            self._loop.run_forever()\n        except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n            logger.exception(\"Error during asyncio-Qt integration tick: %s\", exc)\n            self._loop.create_task(self.crash.capture_exception(exc))\n        finally:\n            # Re-schedule as long as the application is alive\n            if not self._closing.is_set():\n                QTimer.singleShot(15, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def quit(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Can be called by plugins/UI to request a graceful shutdown.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._closing.set()\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Entrypoint                                                                 #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ndef _main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    CLI bootstrap mainly for development/testing purposes.\n    \"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Launch FlockDesk core service\")\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--headless\",\n        action=\"store_true\",\n        help=\"Run without Qt (no GUI) \u2013 suitable for CI or server mode.\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    app = FlockDeskCoreApp(headless=args.headless)\n    app.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    _main()",
    "filePath": "/tmp/project_copy/flockdesk/core/app.py",
    "numLines": 488,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 488
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/tmp/project_copy/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.main\n================================\n\nReal-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\nsubscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\na reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\nimplemented with an MVVM flavour.\n\nThe file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\nFlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\n\nCopyright:\n    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport logging\nimport pkg_resources\nimport signal\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (\n    QApplication,\n    QLabel,\n    QListWidget,\n    QListWidgetItem,\n    QVBoxLayout,\n    QWidget,\n)\n\nLOG = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.presence\")\nLOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Event-Bus Abstractions\n################################################################################\nclass EventBus(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\"\"\"\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass _InProcessBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\n    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\n    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Publish: %s -> %s\", topic, payload)\n        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                await handler(payload)\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                LOG.exception(\"Handler error for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\n        LOG.debug(\"Subscribed %s to %s\", handler, topic)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Model\n################################################################################\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass UserPresence:\n    \"\"\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: str  # e.g. \"online\", \"away\", \"dnd\", \"offline\"\n    last_active: datetime\n    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def is_online(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\"\"\"\n        if self.status in {\"offline\", \"unknown\"}:\n            return False\n        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\n        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# View-Model\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\n    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presence_changed = Signal()\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\n    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\n        return sorted(\n            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\n        )\n\n    @Slot(dict)\n    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\n        emits a notification to the UI controls.\n        \"\"\"\n        user = UserPresence(\n            user_id=evt[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=evt.get(\"display_name\", evt[\"user_id\"]),\n            status=evt.get(\"status\", \"unknown\"),\n            last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt[\"last_active\"]),\n            avatar_url=evt.get(\"avatar_url\"),\n        )\n        LOG.debug(\"Presence updated: %s\", user)\n        self._users[user.user_id] = user\n        self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\"\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Qt View\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\n    It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\n    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._setup_ui()\n        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\n    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\n        self.setWindowTitle(\"Presence\")\n        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n        self._list = QListWidget(self)\n        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n        layout.addWidget(self._list)\n\n    @Slot()\n    def _refresh(self) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Refresh UI\")\n        self._list.clear()\n        for user in self._vm.all_users():\n            item = QListWidgetItem()\n            prefix = \"\ud83d\udfe2\" if user.is_online() else \"\u26ab\"\n            item.setText(f\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\")\n            if user.avatar_url:\n                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\n                if not pixmap.isNull():\n                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\n            self._list.addItem(item)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Presence Service (async)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Background asyncio component responsible for:\n\n        \u2022 Subscribing to the presence topic\n        \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _TOPIC = \"users.presence\"\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._qt_obj = qt_obj\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users\n        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\n\n        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)\n        await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\n        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\n    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\n        thread where the view-model lives.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\n        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\n        LOG.debug(\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\", payload)\n        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self._qt_obj,\n            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\n            Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": self._local_user(),\n            \"display_name\": self._local_user(),  # demo\n            \"status\": status,\n            \"last_active\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"avatar_url\": None,\n        }\n        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\n\n    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(30)\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\n    @staticmethod\n    def _local_user() -> str:\n        # In real life we would ask the identity service\n        return Path.home().name\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceProvider(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\n    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\n    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\n    entry-point.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n\n\ndef _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\n    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\n    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\"flockdesk_presence_provider\"):\n        try:\n            factory = entry_point.load()\n            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n            LOG.info(\"Loaded presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to load presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n    return providers\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Bootstrap\n################################################################################\nasync def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:\n    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\n\n    vm = PresenceViewModel()\n    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\n    widget.resize(240, 400)\n    widget.show()\n\n    # Async service & plugins\n    service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)\n    await service.start()\n\n    for prov in _load_providers():\n        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\n    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\n    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n    qtimer = QTimer()\n    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\n    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\n    qtimer.start()\n\n    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\n\n    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\n    executed directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\",\n    )\n    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        LOG.info(\"Shutting down.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    main()",
    "filePath": "/tmp/project_copy/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py",
    "numLines": 335,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 335
  },
  "type": "text"
}
46. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.main\n================================\n\nReal-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\nsubscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\na reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\nimplemented with an MVVM flavour.\n\nThe file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\nFlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\n\nThis module now uses the unified UserService instead of the old PresenceService.\n\nCopyright:\n    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport logging\nimport pkg_resources\nimport signal\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (\n    QApplication,\n    QLabel,\n    QListWidget,\n    QListWidgetItem,\n    QVBoxLayout,\n    QWidget,\n)\n\n# Import the unified UserService\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService, PresenceState\nexcept ImportError:\n    UserService = None  # type: ignore\n    PresenceState = None  # type: ignore\n\nLOG = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.presence\")\nLOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Event-Bus Abstractions\n################################################################################\nclass EventBus(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\"\"\"\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass _InProcessBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\n    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\n    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Publish: %s -> %s\", topic, payload)\n        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                await handler(payload)\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                LOG.exception(\"Handler error for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\n        LOG.debug(\"Subscribed %s to %s\", handler, topic)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Model\n################################################################################\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass UserPresence:\n    \"\"\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: str  # e.g. \"online\", \"away\", \"dnd\", \"offline\"\n    last_active: datetime\n    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def is_online(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\"\"\"\n        if self.status in {\"offline\", \"unknown\"}:\n            return False\n        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\n        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# View-Model\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\n    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\n\n    Updated to work with UserService for unified user data management.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presence_changed = Signal()\n\n    def __init__(self, user_service: Optional[\"UserService\"] = None) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\n        self._user_service = user_service\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\n    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\n        return sorted(\n            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\n        )\n\n    @Slot(dict)\n    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\n        emits a notification to the UI controls.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Handle both old presence.updated format and new user_service format\n        last_active = evt.get(\"last_active\") or evt.get(\"updated_at\")\n        if isinstance(last_active, str):\n            last_active = datetime.fromisoformat(last_active)\n        elif last_active is None:\n            last_active = datetime.utcnow()\n\n        user = UserPresence(\n            user_id=evt[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=evt.get(\"display_name\", evt.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"display_name\", evt[\"user_id\"])),\n            status=evt.get(\"status\", evt.get(\"state\", \"unknown\")),\n            last_active=last_active,\n            avatar_url=evt.get(\"avatar_url\", evt.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"avatar_path\")),\n        )\n        LOG.debug(\"Presence updated: %s\", user)\n        self._users[user.user_id] = user\n        self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    def update_from_user_service(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update presence data from the UserService.\"\"\"\n        if self._user_service:\n            for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():\n                updated_at = user_view.get(\"status_updated_at\")\n                if isinstance(updated_at, str):\n                    updated_at = datetime.fromisoformat(updated_at)\n                elif updated_at is None:\n                    updated_at = datetime.utcnow()\n\n                user = UserPresence(\n                    user_id=user_view[\"user_id\"],\n                    display_name=user_view.get(\"display_name\", user_view[\"user_id\"]),\n                    status=user_view.get(\"status\", \"offline\"),\n                    last_active=updated_at,\n                    avatar_url=user_view.get(\"avatar_path\"),\n                )\n                self._users[user.user_id] = user\n            self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\"\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Qt View\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\n    It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\n    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._setup_ui()\n        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\n    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\n        self.setWindowTitle(\"Presence\")\n        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n        self._list = QListWidget(self)\n        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n        layout.addWidget(self._list)\n\n    @Slot()\n    def _refresh(self) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Refresh UI\")\n        self._list.clear()\n        for user in self._vm.all_users():\n            item = QListWidgetItem()\n            prefix = \"\ud83d\udfe2\" if user.is_online() else \"\u26ab\"\n            item.setText(f\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\")\n            if user.avatar_url:\n                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\n                if not pixmap.isNull():\n                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\n            self._list.addItem(item)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Presence Module Service (uses UserService internally)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceModuleService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Background asyncio component responsible for:\n\n        \u2022 Interfacing with the unified UserService\n        \u2022 Subscribing to presence events\n        \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\n\n    This replaces the old PresenceService, now delegating to UserService.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _TOPIC = \"presence.updated\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        bus: EventBus,\n        vm: PresenceViewModel,\n        qt_obj: QObject,\n        user_service: Optional[\"UserService\"] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._qt_obj = qt_obj\n        self._user_service = user_service\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        # Subscribe for push updates coming from UserService\n        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\n        # Also subscribe to the old topic for backwards compatibility\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"users.presence\", self._handle_presence)\n\n        # If we have a UserService, start it and set initial status\n        if self._user_service:\n            self._user_service.start()\n            await self._user_service.start_async()\n            # Update VM with initial data\n            self._vm.update_from_user_service()\n        else:\n            # Fallback: Send an initial handshake for the local user\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n            # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\n            asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the service and clean up.\"\"\"\n        if self._user_service:\n            await self._user_service.stop_async()\n            self._user_service.stop()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\n    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\n        thread where the view-model lives.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\n        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\n        LOG.debug(\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\", payload)\n        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self._qt_obj,\n            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\n            Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Fallback presence publishing when UserService is not available.\"\"\"\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": self._local_user(),\n            \"display_name\": self._local_user(),  # demo\n            \"status\": status,\n            \"state\": status,\n            \"last_active\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"updated_at\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"avatar_url\": None,\n            \"metadata\": {},\n        }\n        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\n\n    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Fallback heartbeat when UserService is not available.\"\"\"\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(30)\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\n    @staticmethod\n    def _local_user() -> str:\n        # In real life we would ask the identity service\n        return Path.home().name\n\n\n# Backwards compatibility alias\nPresenceService = PresenceModuleService\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceProvider(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\n    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\n    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\n    entry-point.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n\n\ndef _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\n    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\n    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\"flockdesk_presence_provider\"):\n        try:\n            factory = entry_point.load()\n            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n            LOG.info(\"Loaded presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to load presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n    return providers\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Bootstrap\n################################################################################\nasync def _async_main(bus: EventBus, user_service: Optional[\"UserService\"] = None) -> None:\n    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\n\n    vm = PresenceViewModel(user_service=user_service)\n    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\n    widget.resize(240, 400)\n    widget.show()\n\n    # Async service & plugins - now using PresenceModuleService with UserService\n    service = PresenceModuleService(bus, vm, widget, user_service=user_service)\n    await service.start()\n\n    for prov in _load_providers():\n        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\n    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\n    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n    qtimer = QTimer()\n    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\n    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\n    qtimer.start()\n\n    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\n\n    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\n\n    # Cleanup\n    await service.stop()\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\n    executed directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\",\n    )\n    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\n\n    # Create UserService if available\n    user_service = None\n    if UserService is not None:\n        user_service = UserService(bus, user_id=Path.home().name)\n\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus, user_service))\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        LOG.info(\"Shutting down.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    main()\n",
  "file_path": "/tmp/project_copy/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.main\n================================\n\nReal-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\nsubscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\na reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\nimplemented with an MVVM flavour.\n\nThe file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\nFlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\n\nThis module now uses the unified UserService instead of the old PresenceService.\n\nCopyright:\n    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport logging\nimport pkg_resources\nimport signal\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (\n    QApplication,\n    QLabel,\n    QListWidget,\n    QListWidgetItem,\n    QVBoxLayout,\n    QWidget,\n)\n\n# Import the unified UserService\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService, PresenceState\nexcept ImportError:\n    UserService = None  # type: ignore\n    PresenceState = None  # type: ignore\n\nLOG = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.presence\")\nLOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Event-Bus Abstractions\n################################################################################\nclass EventBus(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\"\"\"\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass _InProcessBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\n    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\n    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Publish: %s -> %s\", topic, payload)\n        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                await handler(payload)\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                LOG.exception(\"Handler error for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\n        LOG.debug(\"Subscribed %s to %s\", handler, topic)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Model\n################################################################################\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass UserPresence:\n    \"\"\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: str  # e.g. \"online\", \"away\", \"dnd\", \"offline\"\n    last_active: datetime\n    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def is_online(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\"\"\"\n        if self.status in {\"offline\", \"unknown\"}:\n            return False\n        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\n        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# View-Model\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\n    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\n\n    Updated to work with UserService for unified user data management.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presence_changed = Signal()\n\n    def __init__(self, user_service: Optional[\"UserService\"] = None) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\n        self._user_service = user_service\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\n    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\n        return sorted(\n            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\n        )\n\n    @Slot(dict)\n    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\n        emits a notification to the UI controls.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Handle both old presence.updated format and new user_service format\n        last_active = evt.get(\"last_active\") or evt.get(\"updated_at\")\n        if isinstance(last_active, str):\n            last_active = datetime.fromisoformat(last_active)\n        elif last_active is None:\n            last_active = datetime.utcnow()\n\n        user = UserPresence(\n            user_id=evt[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=evt.get(\"display_name\", evt.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"display_name\", evt[\"user_id\"])),\n            status=evt.get(\"status\", evt.get(\"state\", \"unknown\")),\n            last_active=last_active,\n            avatar_url=evt.get(\"avatar_url\", evt.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"avatar_path\")),\n        )\n        LOG.debug(\"Presence updated: %s\", user)\n        self._users[user.user_id] = user\n        self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    def update_from_user_service(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update presence data from the UserService.\"\"\"\n        if self._user_service:\n            for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():\n                updated_at = user_view.get(\"status_updated_at\")\n                if isinstance(updated_at, str):\n                    updated_at = datetime.fromisoformat(updated_at)\n                elif updated_at is None:\n                    updated_at = datetime.utcnow()\n\n                user = UserPresence(\n                    user_id=user_view[\"user_id\"],\n                    display_name=user_view.get(\"display_name\", user_view[\"user_id\"]),\n                    status=user_view.get(\"status\", \"offline\"),\n                    last_active=updated_at,\n                    avatar_url=user_view.get(\"avatar_path\"),\n                )\n                self._users[user.user_id] = user\n            self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\"\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Qt View\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\n    It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\n    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._setup_ui()\n        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\n    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\n        self.setWindowTitle(\"Presence\")\n        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n        self._list = QListWidget(self)\n        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n        layout.addWidget(self._list)\n\n    @Slot()\n    def _refresh(self) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Refresh UI\")\n        self._list.clear()\n        for user in self._vm.all_users():\n            item = QListWidgetItem()\n            prefix = \"\ud83d\udfe2\" if user.is_online() else \"\u26ab\"\n            item.setText(f\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\")\n            if user.avatar_url:\n                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\n                if not pixmap.isNull():\n                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\n            self._list.addItem(item)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Presence Module Service (uses UserService internally)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceModuleService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Background asyncio component responsible for:\n\n        \u2022 Interfacing with the unified UserService\n        \u2022 Subscribing to presence events\n        \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\n\n    This replaces the old PresenceService, now delegating to UserService.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _TOPIC = \"presence.updated\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        bus: EventBus,\n        vm: PresenceViewModel,\n        qt_obj: QObject,\n        user_service: Optional[\"UserService\"] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._qt_obj = qt_obj\n        self._user_service = user_service\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        # Subscribe for push updates coming from UserService\n        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\n        # Also subscribe to the old topic for backwards compatibility\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"users.presence\", self._handle_presence)\n\n        # If we have a UserService, start it and set initial status\n        if self._user_service:\n            self._user_service.start()\n            await self._user_service.start_async()\n            # Update VM with initial data\n            self._vm.update_from_user_service()\n        else:\n            # Fallback: Send an initial handshake for the local user\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n            # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\n            asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the service and clean up.\"\"\"\n        if self._user_service:\n            await self._user_service.stop_async()\n            self._user_service.stop()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\n    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\n        thread where the view-model lives.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\n        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\n        LOG.debug(\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\", payload)\n        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self._qt_obj,\n            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\n            Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Fallback presence publishing when UserService is not available.\"\"\"\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": self._local_user(),\n            \"display_name\": self._local_user(),  # demo\n            \"status\": status,\n            \"state\": status,\n            \"last_active\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"updated_at\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"avatar_url\": None,\n            \"metadata\": {},\n        }\n        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\n\n    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Fallback heartbeat when UserService is not available.\"\"\"\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(30)\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\n    @staticmethod\n    def _local_user() -> str:\n        # In real life we would ask the identity service\n        return Path.home().name\n\n\n# Backwards compatibility alias\nPresenceService = PresenceModuleService\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceProvider(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\n    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\n    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\n    entry-point.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n\n\ndef _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\n    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\n    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\"flockdesk_presence_provider\"):\n        try:\n            factory = entry_point.load()\n            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n            LOG.info(\"Loaded presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to load presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n    return providers\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Bootstrap\n################################################################################\nasync def _async_main(bus: EventBus, user_service: Optional[\"UserService\"] = None) -> None:\n    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\n\n    vm = PresenceViewModel(user_service=user_service)\n    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\n    widget.resize(240, 400)\n    widget.show()\n\n    # Async service & plugins - now using PresenceModuleService with UserService\n    service = PresenceModuleService(bus, vm, widget, user_service=user_service)\n    await service.start()\n\n    for prov in _load_providers():\n        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\n    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\n    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n    qtimer = QTimer()\n    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\n    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\n    qtimer.start()\n\n    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\n\n    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\n\n    # Cleanup\n    await service.stop()\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\n    executed directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\",\n    )\n    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\n\n    # Create UserService if available\n    user_service = None\n    if UserService is not None:\n        user_service = UserService(bus, user_id=Path.home().name)\n\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus, user_service))\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        LOG.info(\"Shutting down.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    main()\n",
  "filePath": "/tmp/project_copy/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.main\n================================\n\nReal-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\nsubscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\na reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\nimplemented with an MVVM flavour.\n\nThe file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\nFlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\n\nCopyright:\n    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport logging\nimport pkg_resources\nimport signal\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (\n    QApplication,\n    QLabel,\n    QListWidget,\n    QListWidgetItem,\n    QVBoxLayout,\n    QWidget,\n)\n\nLOG = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.presence\")\nLOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Event-Bus Abstractions\n################################################################################\nclass EventBus(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\"\"\"\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass _InProcessBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\n    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\n    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Publish: %s -> %s\", topic, payload)\n        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                await handler(payload)\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                LOG.exception(\"Handler error for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self,\n        topic: str,\n        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n        *,\n        buffered: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\n        LOG.debug(\"Subscribed %s to %s\", handler, topic)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Model\n################################################################################\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass UserPresence:\n    \"\"\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: str  # e.g. \"online\", \"away\", \"dnd\", \"offline\"\n    last_active: datetime\n    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def is_online(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\"\"\"\n        if self.status in {\"offline\", \"unknown\"}:\n            return False\n        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\n        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# View-Model\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\n    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presence_changed = Signal()\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\n    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\n        return sorted(\n            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\n        )\n\n    @Slot(dict)\n    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\n        emits a notification to the UI controls.\n        \"\"\"\n        user = UserPresence(\n            user_id=evt[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=evt.get(\"display_name\", evt[\"user_id\"]),\n            status=evt.get(\"status\", \"unknown\"),\n            last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt[\"last_active\"]),\n            avatar_url=evt.get(\"avatar_url\"),\n        )\n        LOG.debug(\"Presence updated: %s\", user)\n        self._users[user.user_id] = user\n        self.presence_changed.emit()\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\"\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Qt View\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\n    It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\n    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._setup_ui()\n        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\n    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\n        self.setWindowTitle(\"Presence\")\n        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n        self._list = QListWidget(self)\n        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n        layout.addWidget(self._list)\n\n    @Slot()\n    def _refresh(self) -> None:\n        LOG.debug(\"Refresh UI\")\n        self._list.clear()\n        for user in self._vm.all_users():\n            item = QListWidgetItem()\n            prefix = \"\ud83d\udfe2\" if user.is_online() else \"\u26ab\"\n            item.setText(f\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\")\n            if user.avatar_url:\n                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\n                if not pixmap.isNull():\n                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\n            self._list.addItem(item)\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Presence Service (async)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Background asyncio component responsible for:\n\n        \u2022 Subscribing to the presence topic\n        \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _TOPIC = \"users.presence\"\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._vm = vm\n        self._qt_obj = qt_obj\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users\n        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\n\n        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)\n        await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\n        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\n    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\n        thread where the view-model lives.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\n        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\n        LOG.debug(\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\", payload)\n        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self._qt_obj,\n            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\n            Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": self._local_user(),\n            \"display_name\": self._local_user(),  # demo\n            \"status\": status,\n            \"last_active\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"avatar_url\": None,\n        }\n        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\n\n    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\n        while True:\n            await asyncio.sleep(30)\n            await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\n    @staticmethod\n    def _local_user() -> str:\n        # In real life we would ask the identity service\n        return Path.home().name\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\n################################################################################\nclass PresenceProvider(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\n    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\n    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\n    entry-point.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n\n\ndef _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\n    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\n    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\"flockdesk_presence_provider\"):\n        try:\n            factory = entry_point.load()\n            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n            LOG.info(\"Loaded presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            LOG.exception(\"Failed to load presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n    return providers\n\n\n################################################################################\n# Bootstrap\n################################################################################\nasync def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:\n    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\n\n    vm = PresenceViewModel()\n    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\n    widget.resize(240, 400)\n    widget.show()\n\n    # Async service & plugins\n    service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)\n    await service.start()\n\n    for prov in _load_providers():\n        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\n    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\n    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n    qtimer = QTimer()\n    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\n    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\n    qtimer.start()\n\n    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\n\n    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\n    executed directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\",\n    )\n    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        LOG.info(\"Shutting down.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    main()",
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the",

      "lines": [
        "     QWidget,",
        " )",
        " ",
        "+# Import the unified UserService",
        "+try:",
        "+    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService, PresenceState",
        "+except ImportError:",
        "+    UserService = None  # type: ignore",
        "+    PresenceState = None  # type: ignore",
        "+",

        " LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)",
